option.name: filePath
option.value: /AGENTS (snapshot).md

option.name: originalContent
option.value:
# AGENTS.md — Guide for AI coding agents
Purpose: help automated agents quickly understand, modify, and verify the FakePlayerPlugin codebase.
1) Quick repo map - `src/main/java/me/bill/fakePlayerPlugin/` — main code and entry points - `src/main/resources/` — default `config.yml`, `plugin.yml`, `bot-names.yml`, `bot-messages.yml`, `secrets.yml` (AI API keys template — copied to `plugins/FakePlayerPlugin/secrets.yml` on first run; managed by `SecretsConfig`). Bundled sample personality prompts: `personalities/friendly.txt`, `personalities/grumpy.txt`, `personalities/noob.txt` — extracted to `plugins/FakePlayerPlugin/personalities/` on first run. - `src/main/resources/language/en.yml` — all user-facing messages (loaded by `Lang.java`) - `pom.xml` — build + jar metadata (`target/fpp-*.jar` produced) - `frontend/`, root `package.json`, and `vercel.json` — separate Node/Vercel status + update API sidecar used by the plugin’s update-check fallback; not part of the Maven plugin jar build. - `frontend/wiki/` — primary feature docs (Commands.md, Configuration.md, Getting-Started.md, Database.md, Language.md, Permissions.md, Skin-System.md, Swap-System.md, Bot-Behaviour.md, Bot-Messages.md, Bot-Names.md, Fake-Chat.md, FAQ.md, Migration.md, Home.md, Proxy-Support.md, Config-Sync.md, Placeholders.md, Peak-Hours.md, Changelog.md). Root `wiki/` currently contains a small subset (e.g. Changelog/Proxy/Config-Sync/Placeholders). - `src/main/java/me/bill/fakePlayerPlugin/gui/` — GUI systems: `SettingGui` (in-game settings, `/fpp settings`), `HelpGui` (paginated command GUI used by `/fpp help`; console still uses text fallback), and `BotSettingGui` (per-bot 6-row chest GUI; opened by shift-right-clicking a bot entity when `Config.isBotShiftRightClickSettingsEnabled()` — categories: General (frozen, head-AI, swim-AI, chunk-load-radius, pickup-items, pickup-xp, rename), Chat, PvP (coming-soon overrides), Cmds, Danger; uses `BotRenameHelper` for in-GUI rename; instantiated in `onEnable`, registered as listener, passed to `InventoryCommand` constructor). - `src/main/java/me/bill/fakePlayerPlugin/util/` — helper subsystems and utilities (e.g. `FppMetrics`, `FppLogger`, `FppPlaceholderExpansion`, `TabListManager`, `BackupManager`, `UpdateChecker`, `ConfigValidator`, `DataMigrator`, `TextUtil`, `ConfigMigrator`, `LuckPermsHelper`, `CompatibilityChecker`, `YamlFileSyncer`, `BotTabTeam`, `WorldGuardHelper`, `BadwordFilter`, `BotRenameHelper`, `NameTagHelper`, `FlagParser`, `AttributionManager`, `AttributionApiManager`). **`NameTagHelper`** — integration shim for the NameTag soft-dep; inner records `BotSkin` and `NickData`; `canRename()` helper; **ClassLoader guard:** never call unless `plugin.isNameTagAvailable()` returns `true` (cached in `onEnable` via `Bukkit.getPluginManager().getPlugin("NameTag") != null`). **`FlagParser`** — reusable command arg/flag parser with deprecation aliases, duplicate detection, and conflict detection. Pattern: `new FlagParser(args).deprecate(...).conflicts(...).parse()` — use this in new commands instead of ad-hoc arg scanning. `BotRenameHelper` centralises the validate → delete → respawn → restore lifecycle shared by `RenameCommand` and `BotSettingGui`. **`AttributionManager`** — lightweight attribution protection; validates original author credit in `plugin.yml` on startup via `AttributionManager.validate(plugin)` (called in `onEnable`). Encoded int-array storage prevents trivial text-search edits. Public API: `getOriginalAuthor()`, `getAttributionMessage()`, `getModrinthLink()`, `getGithubLink()`, `quickAuthorCheck()`, `quickMessageCheck()`, `isIntegrityValid()`, `formatAuthors(List<String>)`. Prefers API-verified data from `AttributionApiManager` when available, falls back to built-in encoded values. **`AttributionApiManager`** — async API-based attribution verification; fetches official data from `fakeplayerplugin.xyz/api/attribution` on startup (`init(plugin)` called in `onEnable` after `AttributionManager.validate`). Caches response to `data/attribution-cache.json`; falls back to local cache then built-in encoded data when API unreachable. Public API: `init(Plugin)`, `hasData()`, `isApiReachable()`, `getAuthor()`, `getMessage()`, `getModrinthLink()`, `getGithubLink()`. **`BadwordFilter`** — validates and sanitises bot names. Word sources (merged at load time): (1) remote global baseline (`badword-filter.global-list-url`, default CMU list — enabled by default), (2) `badword-filter.words` inline list in `config.yml`, (3) `plugins/FakePlayerPlugin/bad-words.yml` (local YAML + custom regex `patterns:` block). Detection passes applied in order: raw (case-insensitive substring), leet (after `0→o 1→i 3→e 4→a …` normalisation), aggressive (`auto-detection.enabled: true` — duplicate-char collapse), regex (auto-generated repeat/separator patterns + custom patterns). Whitelist: `badword-filter.whitelist` exact names always pass. Auto-rename: `badword-filter.auto-rename: true` substitutes a random clean name instead of hard-blocking. Public API: `BadwordFilter.reload(plugin)`, `isAllowed(name)`, `sanitize(name)`, `findBadword(name)`, `getBadwordCount()`, `getWhitelistCount()`. Called during startup (`BadwordFilter.reload(this)` after `Config.init`, before `Lang.init`), on every `/fpp reload`, and in `SpawnCommand`/`BotRenameHelper` for per-name validation. - `src/main/java/me/bill/fakePlayerPlugin/listener/` — Bukkit event listeners (`PlayerJoinListener`, `FakePlayerEntityListener`, `FakePlayerKickListener`, `BotCollisionListener`, `PlayerWorldChangeListener`, `ServerListListener`, `BotCommandBlocker`, `BotSpawnProtectionListener`, `BotXpPickupListener`, `BotMessageListener`). Note: `LuckPermsUpdateListener` was removed — LP now detects NMS bots as real players automatically. `BotCommandBlocker` prevents bots from executing any commands. `BotSpawnProtectionListener` prevents lobby/spawn plugins from teleporting bots during their 5-tick spawn grace period. `BotXpPickupListener` gates XP orb pickup (`PlayerPickupExperienceEvent`) and programmatic XP grants (`PlayerExpChangeEvent`) for bots via `Config.bodyPickUpXp()` and the `XpCommand` post-collection cooldown. `BotMessageListener` intercepts `/msg`, `/tell`, `/whisper` (and aliases `/w`, `/m`, `/t`, `/pm`, `/dm`, etc.) directed at bots, cancels the event, and routes to `BotConversationManager`; registered only when `Config.aiConversationsEnabled() && aiProviderRegistry.isAvailable()`. - `src/main/java/me/bill/fakePlayerPlugin/fakeplayer/` — bot domain objects and subsystems (e.g. `FakePlayer`, `FakePlayerBody`, `FakePlayerManager`, `BotPersistence`, `ChunkLoader`, `SkinRepository`, `SkinFetcher`, `SkinProfile`, `SkinProfileInjector`, `NmsHelper`, `NmsPlayerSpawner`, `NmsReflection`, `BotSwapAI`, `BotChatAI`, `BotBroadcast`, `PacketHelper`, `RemoteBotCache`, `RemoteBotEntry`, `BotType`, `BotCombatMode`, `BotPvpAI`, `PeakHoursManager`, `BotPathfinder`, `PathfindingService`, `BotNavUtil`, `StorageInteractionHelper`). **`PathfindingService`** centralises all navigation loops previously duplicated across `MoveCommand`, `MineCommand`, `PlaceCommand`, and `UseCommand`. `Owner` enum: `MOVE`, `MINE`, `PLACE`, `USE`, `ATTACK`, `FOLLOW`, `SYSTEM`. `NavigationRequest` record: `owner`, `destinationSupplier`, `arrivalDistance`, `recalcDistance`, `maxNullPathRecalculations`, `onArrive`, `onCancel`, `onPathFailure`, `lockOnArrival` (optional — when non-null, `cleanup()` atomically calls `manager.lockForAction(botUuid, lockOnArrival)` before firing `onArrive`, eliminating the one-tick gap between nav-lock release and action-lock acquisition). Backward-compatible 8-arg constructor sets `lockOnArrival = null`. Public API: `navigate(fp, request)`, `cancel(uuid)`, `cancelAll()`, `cancelAll(owner)`, `isNavigating(uuid)`, `isNavigating(uuid, owner)`, `getOwner(uuid)`; exposed via `plugin.getPathfindingService()`. **`BotNavUtil`** — static utility class consolidating navigation/interaction helpers shared by `MineCommand` and `PlaceCommand`: `findStandLocation(World, SelectionBounds, tx, ty, tz)` (16-candidate walkable-adjacent search, prefers positions outside the selection), `faceToward(from, target)` (computes yaw+pitch toward a location), `isAtActionLocation(bot, loc)` (XZ ≤ 0.35 proximity check), `useStorageBlock(bot, block)` (NMS right-click via `gameMode.useItemOn`). `SelectionBounds` is an inner `@FunctionalInterface` (`boolean contains(int x, int y, int z)`) so callers pass `sel::contains` without coupling to command internals. **`StorageInteractionHelper`** — encapsulates the lock→open-container→transfer→unlock lifecycle shared by `MineCommand` (deposit bot→storage) and `PlaceCommand` (fetch storage→bot). Static entry point: `interact(fp, faceLoc, block, plugin, manager, transferFn, onFinally)`. Timing: bot locked + teleported on call; +1 tick container opened via NMS; +3 ticks transfer runs, inventory closed, action lock released, GUI refreshed, `onFinally` called. All error paths (bot offline, block no longer a container) also call `onFinally` so callers can clean up gating flags (e.g. `job.depositingToStorage = false`). **BotSwapAI rejoin lifecycle (v1.5.17 critical fix):** rejoin task is now registered AFTER `delete()` completes so `cancel(uuid)` never silently kills the rejoin timer — bots actually come back. New config: `swap.min-online` (floor that prevents swaps if removing one would drop below), `swap.retry-rejoin` (auto-retry failed rejoins, default `true`), `swap.retry-delay` (seconds before retry, default `60`). Bot identification on rejoin: same-name rejoins use `getByName()` (O(1) `nameIndex` lookup); random-name rejoins use UUID diff. Sub-package `fakeplayer/network/` contains the fake Netty layer: `FakeChannel` (discard channel used by `FakeConnection`), `FakeChannelPipeline` (no-op pipeline), `FakeConnection` (NMS `Connection` subclass — overrides all three `send()` variants as no-ops to prevent vanilla connection side-effects during `placeNewPlayer()` that previously caused a phantom "Anonymous User" / UUID-0 ghost entry; `isConnected()` always returns `true`; injects `FakeChannel` and calls `Connection.configureSerialization()` on the no-op pipeline to satisfy NMS internal checks), and `FakeServerGamePacketListenerImpl` (no-op SGPL subclass that discards all outbound packets except knockback). Note: `BotHeadAI` has been removed; head-AI functionality (player tracking, look-range, turn-speed) is now driven by config keys `head-ai.*` and executed inside `FakePlayerManager`. - `src/main/java/me/bill/fakePlayerPlugin/messaging/` — proxy communication (`VelocityChannel`): registers `fpp:main` plugin-messaging channel; wraps outbound messages in a BungeeCord `Forward ALL` envelope so Velocity/BungeeCord re-delivers to every backend. Subchannels: `BOT_SPAWN`, `BOT_DESPAWN`, `BOT_UPDATE` (display-name sync — wire: `[msgId][serverId][uuid][newDisplayName]`; sent by `broadcastBotDisplayNameUpdate(fp)` when LP refreshes a bot's display name), `CHAT`, `ALERT`, `JOIN`, `LEAVE`, `SYNC`. Echo suppression via per-message unique IDs. - `src/main/java/me/bill/fakePlayerPlugin/sync/` — proxy config sync (`ConfigSyncManager`): push/pull config files to/from MySQL in NETWORK mode. Supports `DISABLED`, `MANUAL`, `AUTO_PULL`, `AUTO_PUSH` modes. Server-specific keys (`database.server-id`, `database.mysql.*`, `debug`) are never synced. - `src/main/java/me/bill/fakePlayerPlugin/database/` — session history and analytics (`DatabaseManager`, `BotRecord`). **Schema version 17** — persistent tables: `fpp_bot_sessions`, `fpp_active_bots` (v8→v9 added `frozen`, `chat_enabled`, `chat_tier`, `right_click_cmd`; v9→v10 added `ai_personality VARCHAR(64)`; v10→v11 added `pickup_items BOOLEAN`, `pickup_xp BOOLEAN`; v11→v12 added `head_ai_enabled BOOLEAN DEFAULT 1`, `nav_parkour BOOLEAN DEFAULT 0`, `nav_break_blocks BOOLEAN DEFAULT 0`, `nav_place_blocks BOOLEAN DEFAULT 0`; v13→v14 added `swim_ai_enabled BOOLEAN DEFAULT 1`, `chunk_load_radius INT DEFAULT -1`; v15→v16 added `pve_enabled BOOLEAN DEFAULT 0`, `pve_range DOUBLE DEFAULT 16.0`, `pve_priority VARCHAR(16)`, `pve_mob_type VARCHAR(64)` — per-bot PvE/attack settings; v16→v17 added `skin_texture TEXT`, `skin_signature TEXT` — persists resolved skin data across restarts), **`fpp_skin_cache`** (v14→v15 — persistent resolved-skin cache; `getSkinCacheSize()`, `cleanExpiredSkinCache()` — `cleanExpiredSkinCache()` called in `onEnable` after DB init), `fpp_sleeping_bots` (peak-hours crash recovery), `fpp_bot_identities` (stable name→UUID registry), **`fpp_bot_tasks`** (v12→v13 — bot task persistence: mine/use/place/patrol state survives server restarts; one row per active task type per bot; columns: `bot_uuid`, `server_id`, `task_type` VARCHAR(16) — values `MINE`/`USE`/`PLACE`/`PATROL`/`FOLLOW`, `world_name`, `pos_x/y/z`, `pos_yaw/pitch`, `once_flag`, `extra_str` VARCHAR(256) — patrol route name, `extra_bool` — patrol random flag; PRIMARY KEY `(bot_uuid, server_id, task_type)`), `fpp_meta`. New API: `saveBotTasks(List<BotTaskRow>)` (enqueued write — replaces all rows for this server then inserts fresh), `loadBotTasksForThisServer()` (synchronous read → `List<BotTaskRow>`), `clearBotTasks()` (enqueued delete — called after tasks are loaded to prevent double-restore), `updateBotAiPersonality(String botUuid, String personality)` (enqueued write), `updateBotNavSettings(uuid, navParkour, navBreakBlocks, navPlaceBlocks)` (enqueued write for nav flags), `updateBotAllSettings(uuid, frozen, chatEnabled, chatTier, rightClickCmd, aiPersonality, pickUpItems, pickUpXp, headAiEnabled, navParkour, navBreakBlocks, navPlaceBlocks, swimAiEnabled, chunkLoadRadius)` (enqueued write for all per-bot toggles in one shot). `BotTaskRow` record: `botUuid`, `serverId`, `taskType`, `worldName`, `posX/Y/Z`, `posYaw/Pitch`, `onceFlag`, `extraStr`, `extraBool`. `ActiveBotRow` record now includes: `headAiEnabled`, `navParkour`, `navBreakBlocks`, `navPlaceBlocks`, `swimAiEnabled`, `chunkLoadRadius`, `pveEnabled`, `pveRange`, `pvePriority`, `pveMobType`, `skinTexture`, `skinSignature` (all per-bot override fields). - `src/main/java/me/bill/fakePlayerPlugin/lang/` — message rendering (`Lang`). - `src/main/java/me/bill/fakePlayerPlugin/permission/` — centralised permission registry (`Perm`). - `src/main/java/me/bill/fakePlayerPlugin/ai/` — AI conversation system: `AIProvider` (interface with `ChatMessage` record), `AIProviderRegistry` (selects first available provider; `isAvailable()`, `getActiveProvider()`, `generateResponse()`), `BotConversationManager` (per-bot-player conversation history, DM handling, public-chat reactions, personality resolution, rate limiting; `handleDirectMessage`, `generatePublicChatReaction`, `clearAll()`), `PersonalityRepository` (loads `.txt` files from `plugins/FakePlayerPlugin/personalities/`; `get(name)`, `has(name)`, `getNames()`, `reload()`). Seven concrete providers: `OpenAIProvider`, `AnthropicProvider`, `GroqProvider`, `GoogleGeminiProvider`, `OllamaProvider`, `CopilotProvider`, `CustomOpenAIProvider`. API keys live in `plugins/FakePlayerPlugin/secrets.yml` (managed by `config/SecretsConfig.java`); template extracted from JAR on first run. Accessed via `plugin.getAIProviderRegistry()`, `plugin.getBotConversationManager()`, `plugin.getPersonalityRepository()`. - `config/SecretsConfig.java` — manages `plugins/FakePlayerPlugin/secrets.yml`; exposes API key accessors for all seven AI providers (`openaiApiKey()`, `groqApiKey()`, etc.); `init(plugin)` copies template from JAR on first run; `reload()`. **Never commit real API keys** — the template file is the only version-controlled copy.
2) Where to start (entry points) - `FakePlayerPlugin.java` — plugin lifecycle (`onEnable` / `onDisable`) and orchestration. - `src/main/java/me/bill/fakePlayerPlugin/command/` — command classes (`SpawnCommand`, `DeleteCommand` (registers as `despawn`; subcommands: `<name>`, `all` (requires `Perm.DELETE_ALL`), `--random [num]` (despawn N random bots), `--num <num>` (despawn N oldest bots); **v1.6.3:** bulk operations `all`, `--random`, `--num` are blocked while `manager.isRestorationInProgress()` returns `true` — sends the `delete-restore-in-progress` lang key; single-bot `<name>` despawn is unaffected), `ListCommand`, `HelpCommand`, `InfoCommand`, `ChatCommand`, `SwapCommand`, `ReloadCommand`, `FreezeCommand`, `StatsCommand`, `MigrateCommand`, `TpCommand`, `TphCommand`, `LpInfoCommand`, `RankCommand`, `AlertCommand`, `SyncCommand`, `PeaksCommand`, `SettingCommand`, `InventoryCommand`, `MoveCommand`, `XpCommand`, `CmdCommand`, `MineCommand`, `UseCommand`, `AttackCommand`, `WaypointCommand`, `BadwordCommand`, `RenameCommand`, `PersonalityCommand`, `PlaceCommand`, `StorageCommand`, `PingCommand`, `FollowCommand`) plus `WaypointStore` (named route persistence used by `/fpp waypoint` and `/fpp move --wp`), `StorageStore` (per-bot supply-container registry, `data/storage-points.yml`), `MineSelectionStore` (area selection persistence for `/fpp mine`, `data/mine-selections.yml`). - `command/FppCommand.java` — interface every sub-command implements (`getName`, `getAliases` (default `emptyList()` — used e.g. by `InventoryCommand` for alias `"inv"`), `getUsage`, `getDescription`, `getPermission`, `canUse`, `execute`, `tabComplete`). - `config/Config.java`, `util/ConfigMigrator.java` — configuration pattern and automatic migration. - `fakeplayer/FakePlayerManager.java`, `fakeplayer/BotPersistence.java` — bot lifecycle, persistence and restore. Key new helpers: `requestNavJump(UUID)` — sets a 5-tick jump-hold for navigation (used by `MoveCommand` to prevent the swim-AI from cancelling jumps before physics fires); `clearNavJump(UUID)` — clears the pending jump on despawn. **Action lock API (renamed from mining lock):** `lockForAction(UUID, Location)` — freezes the bot at the given position and suppresses head-AI for the duration of a block interaction (mining, storage deposit/fetch, right-click use); `unlockAction(UUID)` — releases the lock; `isActionLocked(UUID)` — query; `updateActionLockRotation(UUID, float yaw, float pitch)` — updates the bot's stored yaw/pitch while locked (used by `AttackCommand` mob mode for smooth rotation without releasing the lock). Backed by `actionLockedBots` (`ConcurrentHashMap<UUID, Location>`). Previously named `lockForMining`/`unlockMining`/`isMiningLocked` — all old names have been removed. **Navigation lock API (separate from action lock):** `lockForNavigation(UUID)` / `unlockNavigation(UUID)` / `isNavigationLocked(UUID)` — backed by `navLockedBots` (`ConcurrentHashSet<UUID>`). Suppresses head-AI rotation while a bot is actively navigating (PathfindingService calls `lockForNavigation` on `navigate()` start and `unlockNavigation` on `cancel()`). Does NOT freeze bot position — that is the action lock's job. **`bodyTransitionBots`** (Set<UUID>): `isBodyTransitioning(UUID)` — `true` while a bot's NMS body is being swapped (e.g. during world-change re-spawn); prevents duplicate entity registration. **`despawningBotIds`** (`ConcurrentHashMap<UUID, String>`): value is the bot's display name at despawn time; `isDespawning(UUID)` / `getDespawningDisplayName(UUID)` allow event listeners to identify bots being removed and retrieve their display name during the removal window. **`refreshCleanNamePool()`** — pre-filters all names from `bot-names.yml` through the badword filter once; the result is cached in `cleanNamePool` and used by `generateName()` instead of running the expensive regex passes inline. Call this after any badword reload (already done automatically on startup and by `ReloadCommand`). **Entity ID index (v1.6.3):** `entityIdIndex` (`ConcurrentHashMap<Integer, FakePlayer>`) — secondary O(1) lookup by NMS entity ID, maintained at all add/remove/world-change sites. `getByEntity(Entity entity)` checks this first, then falls back to PDC name tag for world-change recovery (and self-heals the index). Public helpers: `removeFromEntityIndex(int entityId)`, `registerEntityIndex(int entityId, FakePlayer fp)` — used by `FakePlayerEntityListener` and `PlayerWorldChangeListener` to keep the index accurate. - `fakeplayer/BotPathfinder.java` — server-side A* grid pathfinder for bot navigation (used by `MoveCommand`). Supports 6 `MoveType` actions: `WALK`, `ASCEND`, `DESCEND`, `PARKOUR`, `BREAK`, `PLACE`. Feature flags passed via `PathOptions` record (`parkour`, `breakBlocks`, `placeBlocks`); all default `false`. Key limits: `MAX_NODES=2000` (plain), `MAX_NODES_EXTENDED=4000` (with advanced options), `MAX_RANGE=64` blocks straight-line. Public entry points: `BotPathfinder.findPathMoves(world, sx,sy,sz, tx,ty,tz, opts)` → `List<Move>`; `BotPathfinder.findPath(...)` → `List<Pos>` (backward-compat, strips action metadata). `walkable(world, x, y, z)` and `passable(world, x, y, z)` are public world-query helpers. - `command/InventoryCommand.java` — `/fpp inventory <bot>` (alias `inv`). Opens a 54-slot double-chest GUI displaying the bot's full inventory (main storage rows 1-3, hotbar row 4, label bar row 5, equipment+offhand row 6). Implements both `FppCommand` and `Listener`. Equipment slots enforce type restrictions (boots/leggings/chestplate-or-elytra/helmet/offhand). Right-clicking a bot entity directly opens the GUI without a command (unless shift-held, which opens `BotSettingGui` instead). `openGui(Player, FakePlayer)` is the public entry point. Constructor: `new InventoryCommand(fakePlayerManager, plugin, botSettingGui)`. Registered as a listener in `onEnable` immediately after command registration. Permission: `Perm.INVENTORY = "fpp.inventory"`. - `command/MoveCommand.java` — `/fpp move <bot|all> <player> | <bot|all> --wp <route> [--random] | <bot|all> --stop`. Navigates a bot (or all bots) to an online player using `PathfindingService`, or sends them on a looping waypoint patrol. Recalculates path when target moves >3.5 blocks or every 60 ticks. Calls `manager.requestNavJump(UUID)` for ascend/parkour/stuck-recovery jumps. Stuck detection (8 ticks without movement) forces jump + recalc. Public `cleanupBot(UUID)` method clears the bot's entries from `activeRouteNames`/`activeRandomFlags`/`randomWpOrder` maps to prevent memory leaks on bot despawn — called by `FakePlayerManager.delete()`. Supports `/fpp move <bot> --stop` to pause navigation. `cancelAll()` is a public method but is NOT called in `onDisable` (nav tasks cancel themselves on arrival or when the bot/target goes offline). Additional public API: `getActiveRouteForBot(UUID)` → route name or `null`; `isRandomPatrolForBot(UUID)` → random-order flag; `resumePatrol(Player, List<Location>, boolean random, String routeName)` — restores patrol state after server restart (called by `BotPersistence`). Permission: `Perm.MOVE = "fpp.move"`. - `command/XpCommand.java` — `/fpp xp <bot>`. Transfers all XP (`getTotalExperience()`) from a bot to the command sender (player only; console gets nothing but XP is still cleared). Clears bot's total XP, level, and progress. Imposes a 30-second post-collection cooldown on the bot's XP pickup (`BotXpPickupListener` reads `plugin.getXpCommand().isOnXpCooldown(uuid)`). Exposed via `plugin.getXpCommand()`. Permission: `Perm.USER_XP = "fpp.xp"` (user-tier, included in `fpp.use`). - `command/CmdCommand.java` — `/fpp cmd <bot> <command...>` (direct execution), `/fpp cmd <bot> --add <command...>` (store right-click command), `/fpp cmd <bot> --clear` (remove stored command), `/fpp cmd <bot> --show` (display stored command). Uses `Bukkit.dispatchCommand()` which bypasses `PlayerCommandPreprocessEvent` so `BotCommandBlocker` does not interfere. Right-click behavior handled in `InventoryCommand.onRightClickBot()` — bots with stored commands execute them instead of opening inventory GUI. Permission: `Perm.CMD = "fpp.cmd"`. - `command/MineCommand.java` — `/fpp mine <bot>` (continuous mining), `/fpp mine <bot> once` (mine one block), `/fpp mine <bot> stop` (stop mining), `/fpp mine stop` (stop all bots). Bots continuously break blocks they're looking at using NMS `handleBlockBreakAction()` and `destroyBlockProgress()` packets. Creative mode: instant break with 5-tick cooldown. Survival mode: progressive mining with destroy-speed accumulation. Mining state tracked per-bot (`MiningState` — `currentPos`, `progress`, `freeze`, `once`). 1-tick repeating task per active miner; tasks auto-cancel when bot/target goes offline. Area-selection mode: `/fpp mine <bot> --pos1`, `--pos2`, `--start`, `--status`, `--stop` — selects a cuboid region and mines it continuously; selections persist across restarts via `MineSelectionStore` (`data/mine-selections.yml`) and auto-resume. Constructor: `new MineCommand(plugin, fakePlayerManager, storageStore, mineSelectionStore, pathfindingService)`. Restocks from nearest registered `StorageStore` container when inventory fills. Public API: `stopMining(UUID)`, `stopAll()`. Permission: `Perm.MINE = "fpp.mine"`. - `command/RenameCommand.java` — `/fpp rename <oldname> <newname>`. Renames an active bot by fully despawning the old entity and respawning it under the new name. Rename lifecycle is now delegated to `BotRenameHelper` (shared with `BotSettingGui`). Preserves all bot state via `BotSnapshot`: inventory (main/armor/offhand, deep-cloned), XP (level + progress + total), chat settings (enabled flag + activity tier), frozen state, LuckPerms group, `aiPersonality`, and original spawner info. **Rename lifecycle:** (1) snapshot captured from old FakePlayer, (2) `manager.markRenaming(oldUuid)` called to suppress join/leave broadcasts, (3) `manager.delete(oldName)` fires, (4) 12 ticks later `manager.spawn(loc, 1, null, newName, true, botType)` called, (5) new bot UUID also marked renaming, (6) `scheduleRestore` polls every 5 ticks until NMS entity ready (up to 200 ticks), (7) snapshot applied, LP group restored 5 ticks later, (8) both UUIDs unmarked, (9) `bot-rename` Lang broadcast sent to all players. The delete+respawn approach guarantees the nametag above the head and death messages reflect the new name (GameProfile name is baked into the NMS entity at spawn time and cannot be changed in-place). **Permission tiers:** `Perm.RENAME = "fpp.rename"` — rename any bot (admin, default: op); `Perm.RENAME_OWN = "fpp.rename.own"` — rename only bots the sender personally spawned (user opt-in, default: false). Tab-complete for arg[0] shows only bots the sender can rename. `fpp.rename` is a parent of `fpp.rename.own` in `plugin.yml` so admins inherit both. **Rename tracking:** `FakePlayerManager.renamingBotIds` (ConcurrentHashSet<UUID>) — both old and new UUIDs are in this set while the rename is in progress. `PlayerJoinListener.onJoin` checks `manager.isRenaming(fp.getUuid())` to suppress the join message for the new entity; `PlayerJoinListener.onQuit` checks `manager.isRenaming(uuid)` in the `isDespawning` branch to suppress the leave broadcast for the old entity. `FakePlayerManager.delete()` also gates the VelocityChannel proxy leave broadcast on `!renamingBotIds.contains(target.getUuid())`. The `bot-rename` Lang key (`{old}` / `{new}` placeholders) is broadcast once when restore completes. - `fakeplayer/PeakHoursManager.java` — time-window-based bot pool scheduler. Runs a 60-second repeating tick; computes a target fraction of the total bot pool that should be online based on the current time window (`peak-hours.schedule` / `peak-hours.day-overrides`), then gradually sleeps (quietly removes) or wakes (respawns) AFK bots to match that fraction. Stagger spread is configured via `peak-hours.stagger-seconds`. Requires `swap.enabled: true`. Integrates with `BotSwapAI` — swapped-out bots count toward the pool so short swap absences don't distort the math. Sleeping-bot state is persisted crash-safely via `fpp_sleeping_bots` DB table (populated on every queue mutation; cleared on `wakeAll()`). Crash recovery: `restoreSleepingBotsFromDatabase(db)` repopulates the in-memory deque at startup. Hot-reload: `reload()` wakes all sleeping bots then restarts the tick if still enabled. Key public API: `start()`, `shutdown()`, `reload()`, `wakeAll()`, `wakeBotByName(name)`, `putBotToSleepByName(name)`, `forceCheck()`, `getSleepingCount()`, `getTotalPool()`, `getSleepingEntries()`, `getSleepingNames()`, `isSleeping(name)`, `getCurrentWindowLabel()`, `computeTargetFraction()`, `getSecondsToNextWindow()`, `getNextWindowFraction()`, `isRunning()`. Accessed via `plugin.getPeakHoursManager()`. Inner record: `PeakHoursManager.SleepingBot(name, loc)`. - `fakeplayer/FakePlayer.java` — domain object for a single active bot (name, UUID, profile, spawnLocation, displayName, skinName, frozen state). `getPhysicsEntity()` and `getPlayer()` both return the single NMS ServerPlayer entity. Additional fields: `luckpermsGroup` (per-bot LP group override, `null` = not yet resolved; get/set via `getLuckpermsGroup()`/`setLuckpermsGroup()`), `rawDisplayName` (the `{bot_name}` slot before LP prefix/suffix are added; used as the stable base for `refreshLpDisplayName` rebuilds), `bodyless` (when `true` the bot exists in tab-list/chat only, no entity in world — used for console spawns without location data), `botType` (`BotType` enum — `AFK` = default passive bot; `PVP` = PvP bot (name prefixed `pvp_<name>`, currently restricted to internal dev UUID); get/set via `getBotType()`/`setBotType()`), `chatEnabled` (per-bot chat mute flag, default `true`; `false` = permanently silenced; get/set via `isChatEnabled()`/`setChatEnabled()`), `chatTier` (per-bot chat activity tier override, `null` = random; values `"quiet"`, `"passive"`, `"normal"`, `"active"`, `"chatty"`; get/set via `getChatTier()`/`setChatTier()`), `aiPersonality` (persistent AI personality name from `personalities/` folder; `null` = use config default; get/set via `getAiPersonality()`/`setAiPersonality()`), `rightClickCommand` (command executed when player right-clicks bot body; `null` = open inventory GUI), `headAiEnabled` (per-bot head-tracking toggle), `pickUpItemsEnabled` / `pickUpXpEnabled` (per-bot item/XP pickup toggles, initialized from `Config.bodyPickUpItems()` / `Config.bodyPickUpXp()`), `navParkour` / `isNavParkour()` / `setNavParkour()` (per-bot pathfinding.parkour override; initialized from `Config.pathfindingParkour()`), `navBreakBlocks` / `isNavBreakBlocks()` / `setNavBreakBlocks()` (per-bot pathfinding.break-blocks override), `navPlaceBlocks` / `isNavPlaceBlocks()` / `setNavPlaceBlocks()` (per-bot pathfinding.place-blocks override), `swimAiEnabled` / `isSwimAiEnabled()` / `setSwimAiEnabled()` (per-bot swim AI toggle; initialized from `Config.swimAiEnabled()`), `chunkLoadRadius` / `getChunkLoadRadius()` / `setChunkLoadRadius()` (per-bot chunk loading radius: `-1` = follow global `chunk-loading.radius`; `0` = chunk loading disabled for this bot; `1-N` = fixed radius capped at global max), `pveEnabled` / `isPveEnabled()` / `setPveEnabled()` (per-bot PvE auto-attack toggle; default `false`), `pveRange` / `getPveRange()` / `setPveRange()` (PvE scan range; initialized from `Config.attackMobDefaultRange()`), `pvePriority` / `getPvePriority()` / `setPvePriority()` (`"nearest"` or `"lowest-health"`; initialized from `Config.attackMobDefaultPriority()`), `pveMobTypes` / `getPveMobTypes()` / `setPveMobTypes()` / `togglePveMobType()` (filtered entity type set; empty = all hostile). All per-bot overrides are persisted by `BotPersistence` and editable in `BotSettingGui`. Note: `botIndex` has been removed; `nametagEntity` remains as a legacy-compat field (no-op for NMS players). - `fakeplayer/FakePlayerBody.java` — NMS ServerPlayer spawning/removal. Entities tagged via PDC keys for identification. - `fakeplayer/NmsPlayerSpawner.java` — reflection-based NMS `ServerPlayer` factory (cached class/method/constructor lookups); bypasses Mojang auth by creating the player directly in JVM memory. - `fakeplayer/NmsReflection.java` — shared NMS reflection bootstrap (CraftBukkit/NMS class resolution); initialised once, thread-safe. - `fakeplayer/PathfindingService.java` — centralised shared navigation service used by all nav-capable commands. `Owner` enum (`MOVE`, `MINE`, `PLACE`, `USE`, `ATTACK`, `FOLLOW`, `SYSTEM`). `NavigationRequest` record fields: `owner`, `destinationSupplier`, `arrivalDistance`, `recalcDistance`, `maxNullPathRecalculations`, `onArrive`, `onCancel`, `onPathFailure`, `lockOnArrival` (optional `Location` — when non-null, `cleanup()` atomically applies `manager.lockForAction()` before `onArrive` fires, eliminating the one-tick gap where neither the nav-lock nor the action-lock is held). Backward-compatible 8-arg constructor sets `lockOnArrival = null`. Public API: `navigate(fp, request)`, `cancel(uuid)`, `cancelAll()`, `cancelAll(owner)`, `isNavigating(uuid)`, `isNavigating(uuid, owner)`, `getOwner(uuid)`. **Nav-lock lifecycle:** `navigate()` calls `manager.lockForNavigation(uuid)` immediately; `cancel(uuid)` calls `manager.unlockNavigation(uuid)`, `manager.clearNavJump(uuid)`, and resets forward motion + sprint + jump flags. Static utility methods: `PathfindingService.xzDist(Location, Location)` and `PathfindingService.xzDistRaw(double, double, double, double)` — XZ-plane distance helpers used by `UseCommand` for proximity checks. Exposed via `plugin.getPathfindingService()`. Constructor: `new PathfindingService(plugin, fakePlayerManager)`. - `gui/BotSettingGui.java` — per-bot 6-row chest GUI opened by shift-right-clicking a bot entity (when `Config.isBotShiftRightClickSettingsEnabled()`). Categories: ⚙ General (frozen toggle, head-AI toggle, swim-AI toggle, chunk-load-radius numeric input, pick-up-items toggle, pick-up-xp toggle, rename action), Chat (chat enabled, tier, AI personality), ⚔ PvP (all "coming soon" per-bot overrides — difficulty, combat-mode, critting, s-tapping, strafing, shielding, speed-buffs, jump-reset, random, gear, defensive-mode), Cmds (set/clear RC command), ⚠ Danger (delete bot). Uses `BotRenameHelper` for the rename action. Instantiated in `onEnable`, passed to `InventoryCommand` constructor, registered as listener. - `command/PersonalityCommand.java` — `/fpp personality [list|reload|<bot> <set <name>|reset|show>]` (alias `persona`). Assigns AI personality files from `personalities/` to bots; persisted to DB via `updateBotAiPersonality()`. Permission: `Perm.PERSONALITY = "fpp.personality"`. - `command/BadwordCommand.java` — `/fpp badword [check|update|status]`. `check` — list all active bots whose names fail the current filter (read-only). `update` — despawn flagged bots and respawn them with random clean names from `bot-names.yml`, fully restoring inventory, XP, chat settings, LP group, and frozen state. `status` — show filter configuration, word counts, and live scan results. Since bot MC usernames are immutable (baked into the NMS `GameProfile`), rename is always despawn+respawn. Permission: `Perm.BADWORD = "fpp.badword"` (default: op, child of `fpp.op`). - `command/UseCommand.java` — `/fpp use <bot> [once|stop] | stop`. Walks the bot to the command sender's position then continuously right-clicks whatever the bot is looking at (blocks, entities, and usable items in air). Supports `once` for a single right-click action, `stop` to stop one bot, and bare `stop` to stop all bots. Internally uses `PathfindingService` (Owner `USE`) to navigate to the sender, then calls `manager.lockForAction()` to hold the bot in place during the use loop. **Persistence API** (used by `BotPersistence`): `getActiveUseLocation(UUID)` → current lock location (null = not using), `isActiveUseOnce(UUID)` → once flag, `resumeUsing(FakePlayer, boolean once, Location)` → restores use mode after restart (navigates to dest first if needed). Public helpers: `stopUsing(UUID)` (cancels task + releases action lock), `stopAll()`, `isUsing(UUID)`, `isNavigating(UUID)`. Permission: `Perm.USE_CMD = "fpp.useitem"`. - `command/AttackCommand.java` — `/fpp attack <bot|all> [--once|--stop|--mob [--range <n>] [--type <mob>] [--priority <mode>]] | --stop`. Two modes: **Classic mode** walks the bot to the command sender's position, locks it there, and continuously attacks entities in the bot's look direction using NMS `nms.attack()` with a manual weapon-cooldown system (mirrors `BotPvpAI.WEAPON_COOLDOWN`); supports `--once` for a single hit. **Mob mode** (`--mob`) is stationary PvE auto-targeting: scans for nearby hostile mobs (or a specific `--type`) within `--range` blocks, smoothly rotates toward the best target (`--priority nearest|lowest-health`), and attacks with proper weapon cooldowns. Re-targets every `Config.attackMobRetargetInterval()` ticks. Never auto-targets players. Uses `PathfindingService` (Owner `ATTACK`) for navigation and `manager.lockForAction()` + `manager.updateActionLockRotation()` during the attack loop. **Config keys** (`attack-mob.*`): `Config.attackMobDefaultRange()` (default `8.0`), `Config.attackMobDefaultPriority()` (default `"nearest"`), `Config.attackMobSmoothRotationSpeed()` (default `12.0` degrees/tick), `Config.attackMobRetargetInterval()` (default `10` ticks), `Config.attackMobLineOfSight()` (default `true`). **Persistence API** (used by `BotPersistence`): `getActiveAttackLocation(UUID)`, `isActiveAttackOnce(UUID)`, `isMobMode(UUID)`, `resumeAttacking(FakePlayer, boolean once, Location)`. Public helpers: `stopAttacking(UUID)`, `stopAll()`, `isAttacking(UUID)`, `isNavigating(UUID)`, `startMobModeFromSettings(FakePlayer)` (called by `BotSettingGui` PvE toggle). `BotPersistence` injection: `setAttackCommand(AttackCommand)` (called in `onEnable` after command creation, before `purgeOrphanedBodiesAndRestore`). Permission: `Perm.ATTACK = "fpp.attack"` (default `true`, child of `fpp.op`). - `command/PlaceCommand.java` — `/fpp place <bot> [once|stop] | stop`. Classic continuous-place mode (like `/fpp mine` but placing): bot stands locked at the sender's position and places blocks it's looking at. Supports `once` for single-placement. **Persistence API** (used by `BotPersistence`): `getActivePlaceLocation(UUID)` → current dest+yaw+pitch lock location (null = not placing), `isActivePlaceOnce(UUID)` → once flag, `resumePlacing(FakePlayer, boolean once, Location)` → restores classic place mode after restart (navigates to dest first if needed). Area-fill mode (`AREA_MODE_ENABLED = false`) is disabled. Area subcommands (`--pos1`, `--pos2`, `--block`, `start`) are blocked while disabled. Permission: `Perm.PLACE = "fpp.place"`. - `command/PingCommand.java` — `/fpp ping [<bot>] [--ping <ms>|--random] [--count <n>]`. Sets the visible tab-list ping of one or all bots. `--ping <ms>` sets a specific latency (0–9999); `--random` assigns random realistic values; no flag shows current ping. `--count <n>` targets N random bots. Permissions: `Perm.PING = "fpp.ping"` (view), `Perm.PING_SET = "fpp.ping.set"` (set specific value), `Perm.PING_RANDOM = "fpp.ping.random"` (random), `Perm.PING_BULK = "fpp.ping.bulk"` (bulk `--count`). All four are children of `fpp.op` in `plugin.yml`. - `command/FollowCommand.java` — `/fpp follow <bot|all> <player> | <bot|all> --stop`. Makes a bot (or all bots) continuously follow an online player using `PathfindingService` (Owner `FOLLOW`). Arrival distance 2.0 blocks; recalculates when target moves >3.5 blocks. On arrival, re-navigates after 5 ticks to maintain continuous following. Supports `--start` (follow sender) and `--stop` (stop one bot or all). Public API: `stopFollowing(UUID)`, `stopAll()`, `isFollowing(UUID)`, `getFollowTarget(UUID)`, `startFollowingFromSettings(FakePlayer, Player)` (called by `BotSettingGui`), `resumeFollowing(FakePlayer, UUID)` (persistence resume), `cleanupBot(UUID)` (called by `FakePlayerManager.delete()`). `BotPersistence` injection: `setFollowCommand(FollowCommand)`. Task type `FOLLOW` persisted to `fpp_bot_tasks`. Exposed via `plugin.getFollowCommand()`. Permission: `Perm.FOLLOW = "fpp.follow"`. - `command/StorageCommand.java` — `/fpp storage <bot> [name|--list|--remove <name>|--clear]`. Registers named supply container locations used by `MineCommand` and `PlaceCommand` for restocking. Persistent via `StorageStore` (`data/storage-points.yml`). Permission: `Perm.STORAGE = "fpp.storage"`. - `command/StorageStore.java` — persistence helper for per-bot `StoragePoint` (name + location) records; `getStorages(botName)`, `addStorage()`, `removeStorage()`, `clear()`. Saved to `data/storage-points.yml`; loaded in `onEnable` before command registration via `storageStore.load()`. - `command/MineSelectionStore.java` — persists mine area selections (pos1/pos2 + active flag) to `data/mine-selections.yml`; selections survive restarts so `/fpp mine <bot> --start` works after a restart without re-selecting corners. Loaded in `onEnable` before command registration via `mineSelectionStore.load()`. - `fakeplayer/RemoteBotCache.java` — thread-safe registry of bots physically running on other proxy servers. Always initialised (even in LOCAL mode). Populated from DB at startup (NETWORK mode) and updated via `BOT_SPAWN`/`BOT_DESPAWN` plugin messages. Exposed via `FakePlayerPlugin.getRemoteBotCache()`. - `fakeplayer/RemoteBotEntry.java` — immutable record (`serverId`, `uuid`, `name`, `displayName`, `packetProfileName`, `skinValue`, `skinSignature`) for a remote bot; `hasSkin()` checks whether texture data is present. - `fakeplayer/SkinProfile.java` — immutable snapshot of a resolved Mojang skin (base64 value + signature + source label). - `config/BotNameConfig.java`, `config/BotMessageConfig.java` — bot name & message pools which are initialised in `onEnable`. - `fakeplayer/SkinRepository.java` / `fakeplayer/SkinFetcher.java` — skin-loading subsystem (lazy singleton; explicitly initialised via `SkinRepository.get().init(this)` in `onEnable` after BotMessageConfig but before database init; must be ready before any bot spawn including persistence restore). - **`fakeplayer/SkinManager.java`** — high-level skin resolution and application layer (v1.6.5+). Instantiated in `onEnable` after `SkinRepository.init`; exposed via `plugin.getSkinManager()`. Key config: `Config.skinMode()` (`skin.mode`, values `"player"` / `"random"` / `"none"`); `Config.skinClearCacheOnReload()`, `Config.skinCustomByName()`, `Config.skinCustomPool()`, `Config.skinUseSkinFolder()`. Public API: `resolveEffectiveSkin(name)`, `applySkinByPlayerName(fp, name)`, `applySkinFromProfile(fp, profile)`, `applyNameTagSkin(fp, botSkin)`, `resetToDefaultSkin(fp)`, `clearCache()`, `getCacheSize()`. - `fakeplayer/network/FakeConnection.java` — NMS `Connection` subclass used for every bot. Created before `placeNewPlayer()` via `new FakeConnection(loopbackAddress)`; sets `this.channel = new FakeChannel(...)` and calls `Connection.configureSerialization()` on the no-op pipeline. Overrides all three `send()` overloads as no-ops to eliminate vanilla connection side-effects (protocol-state registers, pending-ack queues, callback notifications) that caused a phantom "Anonymous User" entry (UUID `0000…0000`) in the player list. `isConnected()` always returns `true` so NMS never treats the bot as disconnected. - `fakeplayer/network/FakeServerGamePacketListenerImpl.java` — extends NMS `ServerGamePacketListenerImpl`; `send()` is a no-op that discards all outbound packets. Exceptions: `ClientboundSetEntityMotionPacket` (knockback) is intercepted and applied server-side via reflected `getMovement()`/`lerpMotion()` (1.21.9+) or `getXa()/Ya()/Za()` fallback. Injected into `ServerPlayer.connection` immediately after `placeNewPlayer()` so `awaitingPositionFromClient` stays `null` — preventing the server from snapping the bot back to its spawn position. - `fakeplayer/SkinProfileInjector.java` — reflection helper that injects a `textures` property into an authlib `GameProfile`; used by both NMS body spawns and tab-list packet profiles for consistent skin application. Call `SkinProfileInjector.apply(gameProfile, skinProfile)`. - `fakeplayer/BotIdentityCache.java` — persistent registry mapping `(bot_name, server_id) → stable UUID`. Ensures a bot with the same name always rejoins with the same UUID (LuckPerms data, inventory, session history stay linked). Storage priority: (1) in-memory `ConcurrentHashMap` (always checked first), (2) `fpp_bot_identities` DB table when DB is enabled, (3) `data/bot-identities.yml` YAML fallback when DB is disabled. Initialized in `onEnable` after the DB, set on `FakePlayerManager` via `setIdentityCache()`; `FakePlayerManager.resolveUuid(name)` delegates here for every fresh spawn. `prime(name, uuid)` pre-populates the cache from `spawnRestored()` so restart-restored bots don't hit the DB a second time. **Identity scope:** per-server — cross-server UUID sharing is intentionally not supported. Keys are lowercased for case-insensitive lookups. - `gui/SettingGui.java` — interactive 3-row chest GUI (`/fpp settings`). 7 categories: General, Body, Chat, Swap, Peak Hours, PvP, Pathfinding; up to 18 settings per page. Toggle items flip their boolean in-place on click; numeric/double items close the chest and prompt the player to type a value in chat (message cancelled — invisible to other players, 60-second timeout auto-cancels). After each change, calls `plugin.saveConfig()`, `Config.reload()`, and `applyLiveEffect(configKey)` — which mirrors `ReloadCommand`'s subsystem calls for instant live effect without a full `/fpp reload`. **Reset button** (slot 18): resets all settings on the current page to JAR defaults. Per-player session state stored in `sessions` map (keyed by UUID). Chat-input sessions tracked separately in `chatSessions`; `pendingChatInput` / `pendingRebuild` sets prevent false `InventoryCloseEvent` handling during transitions. `SettingGui` is a singleton instantiated in `onEnable`, passed to `SettingCommand`, and registered as a listener via `registerEvents(settingGui, this)`. **Permission:** `Perm.SETTINGS = "fpp.settings"` (default `op`). - `lang/Lang.java` — message rendering. Loads `language/en.yml`, supports named and positional placeholders. Use `Lang.get("key", "name", "value")` for an Adventure `Component` or `Lang.raw("key", ...)` for a raw string. Hot-reloadable via `/fpp reload`. - `permission/Perm.java` — central permission registry. All permission nodes declared as constants (e.g. `Perm.SPAWN`, `Perm.DELETE`, `Perm.USER_SPAWN`). Use `Perm.has()`, `Perm.hasOrOp()`, `Perm.resolveUserBotLimit()` helpers. Never hard-code permission strings in commands. - `database/DatabaseManager.java`, `database/BotRecord.java` — session history and analytics. Supports SQLite (default, WAL mode) and MySQL. Writes are serialised on a background thread; positions are batch-flushed on a configurable interval. Controlled by `Config.databaseEnabled()` and `Config.mysqlEnabled()`. **Schema version 17.** Tables: `fpp_bot_sessions` (session history), `fpp_active_bots` (restart persistence source of truth — v8→v9 added `frozen`, `chat_enabled`, `chat_tier`, `right_click_cmd`; v9→v10 added `ai_personality VARCHAR(64)`; v10→v11 added `pickup_items BOOLEAN`, `pickup_xp BOOLEAN`; v11→v12 added `head_ai_enabled BOOLEAN DEFAULT 1`, `nav_parkour BOOLEAN DEFAULT 0`, `nav_break_blocks BOOLEAN DEFAULT 0`, `nav_place_blocks BOOLEAN DEFAULT 0`; v13→v14 added `swim_ai_enabled BOOLEAN DEFAULT 1`, `chunk_load_radius INT DEFAULT -1`), `fpp_sleeping_bots` (peak-hours crash-safe sleeping-bot queue — `sleep_order`, `bot_name`, `world_name`, `pos_x/y/z`, `pos_yaw/pitch`, `server_id`; populated whenever the peak-hours sleep queue changes, cleared on `wakeAll()`), `fpp_bot_identities` (stable name→UUID registry — `bot_name`, `server_id`, `bot_uuid`, `created_at`; PRIMARY KEY `(bot_name, server_id)`; backfilled from earliest session history on schema upgrade), **`fpp_bot_tasks`** (v12→v13 — active task state for mine/use/place/patrol per bot; one row per task type per bot; PRIMARY KEY `(bot_uuid, server_id, task_type)`; saved on shutdown via `saveBotTasks()`, loaded on startup via `loadBotTasksForThisServer()`, cleared after loading via `clearBotTasks()` — same double-restore prevention pattern as `clearActiveBots()`), `fpp_meta` (schema version). Task API: `saveBotTasks(List<BotTaskRow>)`, `loadBotTasksForThisServer()` (synchronous), `clearBotTasks()`. `BotTaskRow` record: `botUuid`, `serverId`, `taskType` (`MINE`/`USE`/`PLACE`/`PATROL`), `worldName`, `posX/Y/Z`, `posYaw/Pitch`, `onceFlag`, `extraStr` (patrol route name), `extraBool` (patrol random). Other API: `saveSleepingBots(List<SleepingBotRow>)`, `loadSleepingBots()`, `clearSleepingBots()`, `lookupBotUuid(botName, serverId)` (synchronous read → `UUID` or `null`), `registerBotUuid(botName, uuid, serverId)` (enqueued write — INSERT OR IGNORE so first UUID always wins), `updateBotAiPersonality(String botUuid, String personality)` (enqueued write), `updateBotNavSettings(uuid, navParkour, navBreakBlocks, navPlaceBlocks)` (enqueued write for the three nav flags only), `updateBotAllSettings(uuid, frozen, chatEnabled, chatTier, rightClickCmd, aiPersonality, pickUpItems, pickUpXp, headAiEnabled, navParkour, navBreakBlocks, navPlaceBlocks, swimAiEnabled, chunkLoadRadius)` (enqueued write — updates all per-bot toggles in one query). `ActiveBotRow` record includes: `headAiEnabled`, `navParkour`, `navBreakBlocks`, `navPlaceBlocks`, `swimAiEnabled`, `chunkLoadRadius`, `pveEnabled`, `pveRange`, `pvePriority`, `pveMobType`, `skinTexture`, `skinSignature`. - `util/TabListManager.java`, `util/UpdateChecker.java` — tab header/footer management and async update checker initialized during startup.
3) Key patterns & concrete examples (copy-ready pointers) - Migration runs before config init: `ConfigMigrator.migrateIfNeeded(this)` in `onEnable` (see `FakePlayerPlugin.java`). - Config usage: call `Config.init(this)` then use accessors like `Config.fakeChatEnabled()` in code. Tab-list visibility accessor: `Config.tabListEnabled()` (key `tab-list.enabled`, default `true`) — when `false`, bots are never added to any player's tab list; the 20-tick display-name refresh loop, `syncToPlayer`, and the respawn re-add in `FakePlayerEntityListener` all check this flag before sending any `sendTabListAdd` packets. Toggling via `/fpp reload` calls `FakePlayerManager.applyTabListConfig()` which immediately removes or re-adds all active bots' tab entries for all online players. **Key accessors:** `Config.fakeChatEnabled()` (`fake-chat.enabled`, default `false`); `Config.fakeChatRequirePlayer()` (`fake-chat.require-player-online`, default `true`); `Config.fakeChatMessages()` (messages from `bot-messages.yml`). Local bots with a body use `player.chat(message)` so the server's real chat pipeline handles formatting. Bodyless bots and remote proxy bots use `Config.fakeChatRemoteFormat()` (`fake-chat.remote-format`, default `"<yellow>{name}<dark_gray>: <white>{message}"`) — supports `{name}` and `{message}` placeholders. `Config.bodyPushable()` (`body.pushable`, default `true`); `Config.bodyDamageable()` (`body.damageable`, default `true`); `Config.bodyPickUpItems()` (`body.pick-up-items`, resource default `true` for new installs — bots pick up items; Java fallback `false`); `Config.bodyPickUpXp()` (`body.pick-up-xp`, default `true` — bots pick up XP orbs; set `false` to prevent all orb pickup; also honoured by the `XpCommand` post-collection cooldown via `BotXpPickupListener`); `Config.dropItemsOnDespawn()` (`body.drop-items-on-despawn`, resource default `true` for new installs; migration v53→v54 adds it as `false` to existing installs — controls whether bots drop inventory + XP when despawned via `/fpp despawn` or the BotSettingGui Danger tab); `Config.warningsNotifyAdmins()` (`messages.notify-admins-on-join`, default `true`) — send compatibility warning to online admins on startup. **Swap system config (v1.5.17):** `Config.swapMinOnline()` (`swap.min-online`, default `0` — minimum bots that must stay online; swap skips if removing one would drop below this floor); `Config.swapRetryRejoin()` (`swap.retry-rejoin`, default `true` — auto-retry failed rejoins); `Config.swapRetryDelay()` (`swap.retry-delay`, default `60` seconds — delay before retry). **Granular debug flags:** `Config.debugStartup()`, `Config.debugNms()`, `Config.debugPackets()`, `Config.debugLuckPerms()`, `Config.debugNetwork()`, `Config.debugConfigSync()`, `Config.debugSkin()`, `Config.debugDatabase()`, `Config.debugChat()`, `Config.debugSwap()` — each maps to `logging.debug.<category>` in config (also triggered by global `debug: true`). **Void message overloads:** every debug flag also has a matching `Config.debugXxx(String message)` variant (e.g. `Config.debugStartup("config.yml loaded.")`) that conditionally calls `FppLogger.debug(topic, enabled, message)` — prefer these overloads at call sites instead of checking the flag inline. **Network/proxy accessors:** `Config.databaseMode()` (`database.mode`, `"LOCAL"` or `"NETWORK"`); `Config.isNetworkMode()` — shorthand for `databaseEnabled() && databaseMode()=="NETWORK"`; `Config.serverId()` (`database.server-id`, falls back to legacy `server.id`); `Config.configSyncMode()` (`config-sync.mode`, one of `DISABLED` / `MANUAL` / `AUTO_PULL` / `AUTO_PUSH`). Server-specific keys (`database.server-id`, `database.mysql.*`, `debug`) are never pushed/pulled by `ConfigSyncManager`. **Performance config (v1.5.17):** `Config.positionSyncDistance()` (`performance.position-sync-distance`, default `128.0` — max distance for position sync packets; `0` = unlimited). Note: `Config.tabListNameFormat()` and the `bot-name.tab-list-format` config key were **removed** in config v38 — LP manages prefix/suffix natively for NMS ServerPlayer entities and the display name packet carries only the bot name itself. **`chunk-loading.radius` behavior (v52→v53):** the value `0` explicitly means no chunk loading (disables all tickets); the value `"auto"` (string) = `Bukkit.getSimulationDistance()` at runtime; `N` (integer ≥ 1) = fixed radius. Old installs that had `0` were migrated to `"auto"` — bots now keep chunks loaded by default matching the server's simulation distance. **Attack mob config (`attack-mob.*`):** `Config.attackMobDefaultRange()` (`attack-mob.default-range`, default `8.0`); `Config.attackMobDefaultPriority()` (`attack-mob.default-priority`, default `"nearest"`); `Config.attackMobSmoothRotationSpeed()` (`attack-mob.smooth-rotation-speed`, default `12.0` degrees/tick); `Config.attackMobRetargetInterval()` (`attack-mob.retarget-interval`, default `10` ticks); `Config.attackMobLineOfSight()` (`attack-mob.line-of-sight`, default `true`). - Language messages: always use `Lang.get("key")` (returns Adventure `Component`) or `Lang.raw("key")` (returns `String`) — never hard-code user-facing text. Named placeholders: `Lang.get("freeze-frozen", "name", fp.getDisplayName())`. All keys live in `src/main/resources/language/en.yml`. - Permission checks: use `Perm.has(sender, Perm.SPAWN)` or `Perm.hasOrOp(sender, Perm.OP)` — never hard-code node strings. **All permissions declared in `Perm.java` as constants AND in `plugin.yml` for LuckPerms auto-discovery.** Per-user bot limits resolved via `Perm.resolveUserBotLimit(sender)` (scans `fpp.spawn.limit.1` … `fpp.spawn.limit.100`; returns -1 if none set, caller falls back to `Config.userBotLimit()`). **Permission structure:** `fpp.op` grants all commands (admin wildcard, default: op); `fpp.use` grants user-tier commands: spawn (with 1-bot limit), tph, xp, info-user (default: true — all players). Bot limits assigned via `fpp.spawn.limit.<num>` nodes (1-100); `fpp.spawn.limit.1` is included in `fpp.use` by default. New command permission constants: `Perm.INVENTORY = "fpp.inventory"`, `Perm.MOVE = "fpp.move"`, `Perm.CMD = "fpp.cmd"`, `Perm.MINE = "fpp.mine"`, `Perm.STORAGE = "fpp.storage"`, `Perm.PLACE = "fpp.place"`, `Perm.PERSONALITY = "fpp.personality"`, `Perm.USE_CMD = "fpp.useitem"`, `Perm.WAYPOINT = "fpp.waypoint"`, `Perm.USER_XP = "fpp.xp"` (user-tier, included in `fpp.use`), `Perm.USER_INFO = "fpp.info.user"`, `Perm.USER_TPH = "fpp.tph"`, `Perm.MIGRATE = "fpp.migrate"`, `Perm.BADWORD = "fpp.badword"`. **New permissions added in v1.6.2+:** `Perm.COMMAND = "fpp.command"` (controls `/fpp` visibility — default `true`; negate to fully hide FPP from a group); `Perm.PLUGIN_INFO = "fpp.plugininfo"` (shows full info panel on bare `/fpp` — default `op`; players without it see only a help hint); `Perm.SPAWN_MULTIPLE = "fpp.spawn.multiple"` (allow multi-bot spawns; child of `fpp.spawn`); `Perm.SPAWN_CUSTOM_NAME = "fpp.spawn.name"` (`--name` flag; child of `fpp.spawn`); `Perm.DELETE_ALL = "fpp.delete.all"` (mass despawn; child of `fpp.delete`); `Perm.NOTIFY = "fpp.notify"` (update notifications on join — default `op`; included in `fpp.op`; grant to non-OP staff). **Ping permissions (v1.6.5+):** `Perm.PING = "fpp.ping"` (view), `Perm.PING_SET = "fpp.ping.set"` (set), `Perm.PING_RANDOM = "fpp.ping.random"` (random), `Perm.PING_BULK = "fpp.ping.bulk"` (bulk). `Perm.ATTACK = "fpp.attack"` (bot attack — classic and mob modes; default `true`, child of `fpp.op`). When adding new commands: (1) add constant to `Perm.java`, (2) add full entry to `plugin.yml` with description and default, (3) add to `fpp.op` children list in `plugin.yml`, (4) use constant in command's `getPermission()` and `canUse()` methods. **LuckPerms tab-completion depends on `plugin.yml` declarations** — missing entries won't appear in `/lp permission set fpp.<tab>`. - Command registration: plugin registers commands via `commandManager.register(new SpawnCommand(...))`; `HelpCommand` is auto-registered inside `CommandManager`, and `/fpp help` reads live from the registered command list, so new sub-commands need no separate help-menu wiring. The plugin then calls `getCommand("fpp").setExecutor(commandManager)` (see `onEnable`). - Listener registration: `getServer().getPluginManager().registerEvents(new PlayerJoinListener(this, fakePlayerManager), this)` — listeners receive plugin + manager references. - Persistence lifecycle: restore on enable via `botPersistence.purgeOrphanedBodiesAndRestore(fakePlayerManager)` (purges orphaned entities then calls `restore()` 5 ticks later) and save on disable via `botPersistence.save(fakePlayerManager.getActivePlayers())`. `BotPersistence.saveAsync()` also available for non-shutdown saves (snapshots locations on main thread, writes async). When DB is enabled, the `fpp_active_bots` table is the **primary** restore source (`DatabaseManager.getActiveBotsForThisServer()`); `data/active-bots.yml` is the fallback for no-DB or empty-table cases. **Task persistence (v1.6.2+):** active tasks (mine/use/place/patrol) are saved to `fpp_bot_tasks` (DB primary) and `data/bot-tasks.yml` (YAML fallback) on shutdown via `writeTaskSnapshot()`; loaded back in `loadTasksFile()` which tries DB first, falls back to YAML. `clearBotTasks()` is called immediately after loading so tasks don't re-restore on the next restart. **Per-bot field persistence:** `BotPersistence` YAML also persists `swim-ai-enabled`, `chunk-load-radius`, `nav-parkour`, `nav-break-blocks`, `nav-place-blocks` per bot (alongside the existing `pickup-items`, `pickup-xp`, `head-ai-enabled`, `frozen`, `chat-enabled`, `chat-tier`, `ai-personality`, `right-click-cmd`). `BotPersistence` injections (must happen AFTER all commands created, BEFORE `purgeOrphanedBodiesAndRestore`): `setMoveCommand`, `setMineCommand`, `setPlaceCommand`, `setUseCommand`, `setAttackCommand`, `setFollowCommand`, `setWaypointStore`. - **Performance optimization (v1.5.17):** `FakePlayerManager.getByName()` is now O(1) via secondary `nameIndex` map (`ConcurrentHashMap<String, FakePlayer>`, lowercased keys) — was O(n) linear scan. The index is maintained at all add/remove sites. Additionally, position sync packets are distance-culled via `Config.positionSyncDistance()` (`performance.position-sync-distance`, default `128.0` blocks, `0` = unlimited) — only players within range receive per-tick position updates, reducing packet overhead on large servers. - Metrics & soft-deps: metrics are initialized with `FppMetrics` when `Config.metricsEnabled()`; PlaceholderAPI expansion registered conditionally in `onEnable`. - Compatibility checks: `CompatibilityChecker.check()` (in `util/CompatibilityChecker.java`) **always returns a non-restricted result** — the old Paper-detection and minimum MC version gate has been removed. `check()` is **not called in `onEnable`**; `compatibilityRestricted` and `compatibilityWarningMessage` fields have been removed from `FakePlayerPlugin`. The class retains two general-purpose utilities: `CompatibilityChecker.extractMcVersion()` (returns detected MC version string) and `CompatibilityChecker.isVersionAtLeast(version, required)` (component-wise numeric comparison). `buildWarningComponent(result)` always returns `null`. **Version gate (separate from `check()`):** `FakePlayerPlugin` directly calls `CompatibilityChecker.isVersionAtLeast(detectedMcVersion, "1.21.12")` in `onEnable` to set `versionUnsupported = true` when the server runs MC 1.21.12+. New fields: `versionUnsupported` (boolean) and `detectedMcVersion` (String); new accessors: `plugin.isVersionUnsupported()` and `plugin.getDetectedMcVersion()`. When `versionUnsupported = true`: `CommandManager` blocks all sub-commands (bare `/fpp` still shows plugin info), `PlayerJoinListener` sends the `"version-unsupported-admin"` Lang warning to operators on every join, and a prominent banner is printed to the console at startup. FPP is tested up to MC 1.21.11; any newer version triggers this gate. - Bot pools: `BotNameConfig.init(this)` and `BotMessageConfig.init(this)` are called during startup — the plugin loads `bot-names.yml` and `bot-messages.yml` early. - Metrics / FastStats packaging: `FppMetrics` is initialized before the startup banner to reflect metrics status. FastStats jars are bundled under `src/main/resources/faststats/` (the POM treats them as binary resources, not shaded). Note: building requires JDK 21 (see `pom.xml` <java.version>21). - Soft-dependencies declared in `plugin.yml`: `PlaceholderAPI`, `LuckPerms`, `WorldGuard`, `NameTag` (all optional, auto-detected). Current `pom.xml` does not declare PacketEvents. - Entity stack: each bot is a single NMS ServerPlayer entity (created via `NmsPlayerSpawner`) with full player functionality including chat, tab-list presence, and realistic movement. Use PDC keys for identification during event handling. - Database gate: check `Config.databaseEnabled()` before any `DatabaseManager` call; `databaseManager` may be `null` at runtime (disabled or failed to init). `databaseManager.recordAllShutdown()` must be called in `onDisable` before `databaseManager.close()`. - Text formatting: `TextUtil.colorize(string)` parses MiniMessage tags and legacy `&`/`§` codes into an Adventure `Component`. `TextUtil.format(string)` additionally applies small-caps Unicode conversion. **Supports all modern color formats:** MiniMessage tags (`<rainbow>`, `<gradient:#FF0000:#0000FF>`), hex colors (`<#9782ff>`), LuckPerms gradient shorthand (`{#fffff>}text{#00000<}`), and mixed formats (`&7[<#9782ff>Phantom</#9782ff>&7]`). - LuckPerms integration: `LuckPermsHelper` reads resolved prefix/suffix directly from LP's in-memory `CachedMetaData` — bots are real NMS `ServerPlayer` entities, LP handles them natively. Core helpers: `LuckPermsHelper.isAvailable()`, `LuckPermsHelper.groupExists(groupName)`, `LuckPermsHelper.getAllGroupNames()`, `LuckPermsHelper.buildGroupSummary()`. Runtime group assignment: `LuckPermsHelper.setPlayerGroup(uuid, groupName)` (works online or offline) or `LuckPermsHelper.applyGroupToOnlineUser(uuid, groupName)` (prefers the live `User` instance so `UserDataRecalculateEvent` fires immediately — preferred when bot is already spawned). Prefix/suffix reads: `LuckPermsHelper.getResolvedPrefix(uuid)` / `LuckPermsHelper.getResolvedSuffix(uuid)` (synchronous, requires bot to be online). Async group read from storage: `LuckPermsHelper.getStoredPrimaryGroup(uuid)`. Online-only read: `LuckPermsHelper.getPrimaryGroup(uuid)`. Force LP metadata refresh: `LuckPermsHelper.refreshUserCache(uuid)`. `LuckPermsHelper.invalidateCache()` is `@Deprecated` (no-op — LP handles caching natively). **Event subscription:** `LuckPermsHelper.subscribeLpEvents(plugin, manager)` subscribes to `UserDataRecalculateEvent` so bot display names auto-update whenever a group changes (LP command, API, etc.); called directly in `onEnable` (not deferred). `LuckPermsHelper.unsubscribeLpEvents()` is called in `onDisable` to prevent memory leaks. **LP group assignment:** `Config.luckpermsDefaultGroup()` (`luckperms.default-group`, default `""`) — optional LP group assigned at spawn; blank = `"default"`. Use `/fpp lpinfo [bot-name]` for in-game diagnostics. Check LP availability via `LuckPermsHelper.isAvailable()` before any API call. **LP injection flow:** `finishSpawn` calls `LuckPermsHelper.ensureGroupBeforeSpawn(botUuid, cfgGroup)` **async before the NMS body spawns** — writes the group to LP storage so `PlayerJoinEvent` listeners (TAB plugin, etc.) see the correct group immediately. If the bot already has a non-default group in storage (prior `/fpp rank`), that group is preserved unless `luckperms.default-group` overrides it. Once LP completes, `spawnBodyAndFinish(fp, spawnLoc)` runs on the main thread. After the body spawns, `LuckPermsHelper.applyGroupToOnlineUser(uuid, group)` is called 5 ticks later; after that completes, `refreshLpDisplayName(fp)` is called 2 ticks later to update the tab-list display name. `FakePlayer.rawDisplayName` is the stable `{bot_name}` base for rebuilds. Call `FakePlayerManager.refreshLpDisplayName(fp)` to manually rebuild display name + resend tab-list update packets. **Scoreboard team override:** All bots are placed in the `~fpp` scoreboard team (managed by `BotTabTeam`) which sorts AFTER all other teams, ensuring bots always appear below real players regardless of LP group weight. Note: `LpData` record, `getBotLpData()`, `buildPacketProfileName()`, `invalidateGroup()`, `refreshRankList()`, and the packet-prefix-char weight-rank ordering system have all been removed. **ClassLoader guard:** `FakePlayerPlugin` caches a `luckPermsAvailable` boolean (set once in `onEnable()` via `Bukkit.getPluginManager().getPlugin("LuckPerms") != null`) accessible via `plugin.isLuckPermsAvailable()`. **Never reference `LuckPermsHelper` or any LP API class unless this returns `true`** — Paper's `PluginClassLoader` eagerly resolves LP API class references during bytecode verification, so even calling `LuckPermsHelper.isAvailable()` on a server without LP installed throws `NoClassDefFoundError`. The pattern everywhere LP is needed: `if (plugin.isLuckPermsAvailable()) { LuckPermsHelper.doSomething(...); }`. - WorldGuard integration: `WorldGuardHelper` (in `util/WorldGuardHelper.java`) provides PvP region protection for bots. Key methods: `WorldGuardHelper.isPvpAllowed(location)` — queries active WG region flags; returns `true` when no region explicitly sets PVP to DENY (fail-open: unprotected areas always allow PvP, only explicit DENY blocks damage); `WorldGuardHelper.findSafeLocation(world)` — returns the world spawn if PvP is allowed there, otherwise `null`. Used by `FakePlayerEntityListener` at `LOWEST` priority to cancel player-sourced PvP damage to bots inside no-PvP regions. **ClassLoader guard:** `FakePlayerPlugin` caches `worldGuardAvailable` (set once via `Bukkit.getPluginManager().getPlugin("WorldGuard") != null`) accessible via `plugin.isWorldGuardAvailable()`. **Never reference `WorldGuardHelper` unless this returns `true`** — same NoClassDefFoundError risk as LuckPerms. Pattern: `if (plugin.isWorldGuardAvailable()) { WorldGuardHelper.doSomething(...); }`. `WorldGuard` is declared in `plugin.yml` as a soft-depend. - NameTag integration: `NameTagHelper` (in `util/NameTagHelper.java`) — shim for the NameTag soft-dep. Inner records `BotSkin` and `NickData`; `canRename()` helper. **ClassLoader guard:** cache `nameTagAvailable` boolean on `FakePlayerPlugin` (set in `onEnable` via `Bukkit.getPluginManager().getPlugin("NameTag") != null`); accessed via `plugin.isNameTagAvailable()`. **Never reference `NameTagHelper` unless this returns `true`**. New config section: `Config.nameTagBlockNickConflicts()` (`nametag-integration.block-nick-conflicts`, default `true` — blocks spawn if a NameTag nick already uses that name), `Config.nameTagIsolation()` (`nametag-integration.isolation`, default `true`), `Config.nameTagSyncNickAsRename()` (`nametag-integration.sync-nick-as-rename`, default `false`). New lang key: `spawn-name-taken-nick`. - Listener registration: `getServer().getPluginManager().registerEvents(new PlayerJoinListener(this, fakePlayerManager), this)` — listeners receive plugin + manager references. `FakePlayerKickListener` (guards all bots from automated kicks like anti-idle/anti-AFK) is always registered; all other listeners (`FakePlayerEntityListener`, `BotCollisionListener`, etc.) are also always registered — the old `compatibilityRestricted` skip is gone. - `onDisable` sequence: `chunkLoader.releaseAll()` → `botChatAI.cancelAll()` → `botConversationManager.clearAll()` → **`peakHoursManager.shutdown()`** (wakes all sleeping bots so they appear in `activePlayers`) → `botSwapAI.cancelAll()` → **save persistence** (sleeping bots are now active and captured) → `LuckPermsHelper.unsubscribeLpEvents()` → `fakePlayerManager.removeAllSync()` (also calls `pvpAI.cancelAll()` internally) → `tabListManager.shutdown()` → `botTabTeam.destroy()` → `databaseManager.recordAllShutdown()` + `close()` → `fppMetrics.shutdown()` → unregister VelocityChannel messaging channels. **Critical ordering:** `peakHoursManager.shutdown()` MUST run before `botPersistence.save()` so sleeping bots are woken and included in the persistence file. - Update notifications to late-joining admins: stored as a `Component` on `FakePlayerPlugin.getUpdateNotification()` / `setUpdateNotification()`; `PlayerJoinListener` delivers it via `Perm.hasOrOp(player, Perm.OP)`. - YAML file sync: `YamlFileSyncer.syncMissingKeys(plugin, diskRelPath, jarResourcePath)` — non-destructive; adds keys present in the JAR but absent from disk, never overwrites user values. Returns a `SyncResult` record (`fileName`, `keysAdded`, `hasChanges()`, `count()`). Called automatically inside `Lang.reload()`, `BotNameConfig.reload()`, and `BotMessageConfig.reload()` on every startup and `/fpp reload`. Force-run from the command line with `/fpp migrate lang`, `/fpp migrate names`, or `/fpp migrate messages`. When adding new keys to `en.yml`, `bot-names.yml`, or `bot-messages.yml`, they will be automatically synced to existing user files on next reload — no manual migration step needed. - Spawn cooldown: `Config.spawnCooldown()` (key `spawn-cooldown`, default `0` = disabled). Applies to both admin and user `/fpp spawn`. Players with `Perm.BYPASS_COOLDOWN` (`fpp.bypass.cooldown`) are exempt. Cooldown state lives in `FakePlayerManager.spawnCooldowns` (per-UUID map). Helpers: `manager.isOnCooldown(uuid)`, `manager.getRemainingCooldown(uuid)` (seconds), `manager.recordSpawnCooldown(uuid)`. - Backup variants: `BackupManager.createFullBackup(plugin, reason)` — backs up all plugin files including SQLite; used by `ConfigMigrator` before migrations. `BackupManager.createConfigFilesBackup(plugin, reason)` — backs up YAML files only (`config.yml`, `bot-names.yml`, `bot-messages.yml`, `language/`); used before lightweight sync operations (e.g. `/fpp migrate lang`). Both methods prune to the most recent `MAX_BACKUPS` (10) automatically. - Additional `Perm` helpers: `Perm.missing(sender, node)` returns `true` when the sender lacks the node (negation of `has()`); `Perm.hasAny(sender, node1, node2, ...)` returns `true` when the sender has at least one of the listed nodes. - Physical-body API: `FakePlayerManager.physicalBodiesEnabled()` — returns `Config.spawnBody()`. (The old `&& !plugin.isCompatibilityRestricted()` check has been removed.) Use this instead of checking `Config.spawnBody()` inline when deciding whether NMS ServerPlayer bodies are active. - Display-name pipeline: `FakePlayerManager.finalizeDisplayName(rawName, botName)` — private helper that expands PAPI `%placeholders%` via `PlaceholderAPI.setPlaceholders(null, ...)` (null player = server-wide), then calls `sanitizeDisplayName()`. **Note:** the `{prefix}/{bot_name}/{suffix}` tab-list format substitution (previously driven by `bot-name.tab-list-format`) was removed in config v38 — LP manages prefix/suffix natively for real NMS ServerPlayer entities; the display name packet carries only the bot name. Called at every display-name build site: `spawnUserBot`, `spawn`, `spawnRestored`, `validateUserBotNames`. Public entry point for post-LP-assignment rebuilds: `FakePlayerManager.refreshLpDisplayName(fp)` (also has a private retry variant `refreshLpDisplayNameWithRetry` for LP metadata race conditions). - Body damageable/pushable live-reload: `FakePlayerManager.applyBodyConfig()` — called on `/fpp reload`; handles damage/pushable configuration for all active NMS ServerPlayer entities. `BotCollisionListener` also guards all three push paths (`onEntityDamageByEntity`, `onPlayerMove`, `tickBotSeparation`) with `if (!Config.bodyPushable()) return;`. - Swim AI: `Config.swimAiEnabled()` (`swim-ai.enabled`, default `true`). Each tick the main loop calls `NmsPlayerSpawner.setJumping(bot, bot.isInWater() || bot.isInLava())` so bots automatically swim upward when submerged, mimicking a player holding spacebar. Set `swim-ai.enabled: false` to let bots sink/drown instead. **Head-AI tick rate:** `Config.headAiTickRate()` (`head-ai.tick-rate`, default `3` — runs every N ticks; lower = more responsive tracking but higher CPU cost). **Collision tuning config:** `Config.collisionWalkRadius()` (`collision.walk-radius`, default `0.85`), `collisionWalkStrength()` (`collision.walk-strength`, default `0.22`), `collisionMaxHoriz()` (`collision.max-horizontal-speed`, default `0.30`), `collisionHitStrength()` (`collision.hit-strength`, default `0.45`), `collisionHitMaxHoriz()` (`collision.hit-max-horizontal-speed`, default `0.80`), `collisionBotRadius()` (`collision.bot-radius`, default `0.90`), `collisionBotStrength()` (`collision.bot-strength`, default `0.14`). **Spawn presets:** `Config.spawnCountPresetsAdmin()` (`limits.spawn-presets`, default `[1, 5, 10, 15, 20]`) — list of preset counts shown in the SpawnCommand GUI/tab-completion for admins. - Pathfinding config (used by `/fpp move`, `PathfindingService`): `Config.pathfindingParkour()` (`pathfinding.parkour`, default `false`); `Config.pathfindingBreakBlocks()` (`pathfinding.break-blocks`, default `false`); `Config.pathfindingPlaceBlocks()` (`pathfinding.place-blocks`, default `false`); `Config.pathfindingPlaceMaterial()` (`pathfinding.place-material`, default `"DIRT"` — material placed when bridging gaps; falls back to `DIRT` if the value is not a valid solid block). These correspond to `BotPathfinder.PathOptions` fields and are re-read on every path recalculation. Additional tuning keys (all re-read at runtime): `Config.pathfindingArrivalDistance()` (`pathfinding.arrival-distance`, default `1.2`), `Config.pathfindingPatrolArrivalDistance()` (`pathfinding.patrol-arrival-distance`, default `1.5`), `Config.pathfindingWaypointArrivalDistance()` (`pathfinding.waypoint-arrival-distance`, default `0.65`), `Config.pathfindingSprintDistance()` (`pathfinding.sprint-distance`, default `6.0`), `Config.pathfindingFollowRecalcDistance()` (`pathfinding.follow-recalc-distance`, default `3.5`), `Config.pathfindingRecalcInterval()` (`pathfinding.recalc-interval`, default `60` ticks), `Config.pathfindingStuckTicks()` (`pathfinding.stuck-ticks`, default `8`), `Config.pathfindingStuckThreshold()` (`pathfinding.stuck-threshold`, default `0.04` blocks/tick), `Config.pathfindingBreakTicks()` (`pathfinding.break-ticks`, default `15`), `Config.pathfindingPlaceTicks()` (`pathfinding.place-ticks`, default `5`), `Config.pathfindingMaxRange()` (`pathfinding.max-range`, default `64`), `Config.pathfindingMaxNodes()` (`pathfinding.max-nodes`, default `2000`), `Config.pathfindingMaxNodesExtended()` (`pathfinding.max-nodes-extended`, default `4000`), `Config.pathfindingMaxFall()` (`pathfinding.max-fall`, default `3` — max blocks a bot will fall during pathfinding). - PVP AI subsystem: `BotPvpAI` drives all movement and combat for `BotType.PVP` bots. Initialized in `FakePlayerManager` constructor; accessed via `manager.getPvpAI()`. The main tick loop calls `pvpAI.tickBot(fp, bot, online)` each game tick for PVP bots only (head-AI is skipped for PVP bots — `BotPvpAI` handles rotation internally). Key config: `Config.pvpAiDifficulty()` (`pvp-ai.difficulty`, default `"medium"`, values: `easy`/`medium`/`hard`/`tier1`/`hacker`); `Config.pvpAiDefensiveMode()` (`pvp-ai.defensive-mode`, default `true` — bots only attack back when hit); `Config.pvpAiDetectRange()` (`pvp-ai.detect-range`, default `32.0`). When a player damages a PVP bot, `FakePlayerEntityListener` calls `manager.getPvpAI().onBotAttacked(botUuid, attackerUuid)` to register the attacker for defensive retaliation. Combat mode (`BotCombatMode.CRYSTAL`/`SWORD`/`FIST`) is auto-detected from the bot's inventory every 2 seconds. Difficulty controls attack range, crit/s-tap consistency, and early-swing window. **⚠ PVP bots are currently restricted to the internal developer UUID — not yet available for general spawning.** - BotChatAI expanded features: Each bot is assigned a random activity tier at first use — quiet (15%), passive (25%), normal (30%), active (18%), chatty (12%) — which scales the chat interval multiplier. Override per-bot via `FakePlayer.setChatTier(String)` or via `/fpp chat <bot> tier <tier>`; reset to random with `tier reset`. Silence a bot permanently (`/fpp chat <bot> off` or `fp.setChatEnabled(false)`) or temporarily (`/fpp chat <bot> mute [seconds]` — `BotChatAI.timedMute(uuid, seconds)`). Inspect state with `/fpp chat <bot> info` (shows enabled, tier, multiplier). Bulk operations: `/fpp chat all <on|off|tier <tier>|mute [seconds]>`. **Chat realism config (v39→v40):** `Config.fakeChatTypingDelay()` (`fake-chat.typing-delay`, default `true`), `Config.fakeChatBurstChance()` (`fake-chat.burst-chance`, default `0.12`), `Config.fakeChatBurstDelayMin()/Max()` (`fake-chat.burst-delay.min/max`, default `2/5`), `Config.fakeChatReplyToMentions()` (`fake-chat.reply-to-mentions`, default `true`), `Config.fakeChatMentionReplyChance()` (`fake-chat.mention-reply-chance`, default `0.65`), `Config.fakeChatReplyDelayMin()/Max()` (`fake-chat.reply-delay.min/max`, default `2/8`), `Config.fakeChatStaggerInterval()` (`fake-chat.stagger-interval`, default `3`), `Config.fakeChatActivityVariation()` (`fake-chat.activity-variation`, default `true`), `Config.fakeChatHistorySize()` (`fake-chat.history-size`, default `5`).
