# ── Bot Body ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── # The physical entity that appears in the world. body: enabled: true # false = tab-list / player-count only, no entity in world pushable: true # true = players and explosions can push the body damageable: true # true = body can take ALL damage (player + environmental) # false = immune to player/entity damage only (fall, fire, etc. still apply) pick-up-items: false # true = bots pick up items from the ground like a real player pick-up-xp: true # true = bots pick up XP orbs from the ground like a real player
# ── Combat ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── combat: max-health: 20.0 # Bot max HP. 20.0 = standard player (2 HP = 1 heart). hurt-sound: true # Play the hurt sound when a bot takes damage.
# ── Death & Respawn ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── death: respawn-on-death: false # true = bot respawns at its spawn location after dying respawn-delay: 15 # Ticks before respawn (20 = 1 s · 60 = 3 s · 100 = 5 s) suppress-drops: false # false = bots drop items/xp on death like real players; true = suppress all drops
# ── Chunk Loading ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── # Bots keep surrounding chunks loaded exactly like a real player. # Mobs spawn, redstone ticks, and crops grow inside the loaded radius. chunk-loading: enabled: true radius: 0 # Chunk radius to keep loaded. 0 = match server simulation-distance. update-interval: 20 # Ticks between position re-checks. 20 = 1 second. # Lower = more responsive, higher = less overhead.
# ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ # 4 · AI SYSTEMS # ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# ── Head AI ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── # Bots smoothly rotate to look at the nearest visible player within range. head-ai: enabled: true look-range: 8.0 # Detection radius in blocks turn-speed: 0.3 # Rotation smoothing — 0.0 = frozen · 1.0 = instant snap tick-rate: 3 # Run the scan + raycast every N ticks instead of every tick. # Higher = lower CPU cost. 3 ≈ 7 scans/s, imperceptible to players. # Range: 1 (every tick) to 20 (once per second).
# ── Swim AI ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── # Bots automatically swim upward when submerged, mimicking a player holding Jump. swim-ai: enabled: true # false = bots sink and drown instead of swimming up
# ── Collision & Push ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── # Controls how hard players push bots on contact and how bots separate from each other. # Defaults work well for most servers — only change if you notice stacking issues. collision: walk-radius: 0.85 # Radius (blocks) at which walking into a bot pushes it walk-strength: 0.22 # Push force when a player walks into a bot hit-strength: 0.45 # Knockback force on a direct hit hit-max-horizontal-speed: 0.80 # Max horizontal speed from hit / explosion knockback bot-radius: 0.90 # Radius for bot-vs-bot separation bot-strength: 0.14 # Separation force applied between two bots max-horizontal-speed: 0.30 # Push speed cap for walk and separation sources
# ── PVP AI · ⚠ COMING SOON ───────────────────────────────────────────────── # Restricted to internal testing — all settings below are locked to their defaults. # Editing them has no effect until the PVP system is publicly released. pvp-ai: pvp: false # Master switch. Forced false — dev-only until public release.
# Core difficulty: medium # easy · medium · hard · tier1 · hacker combat-mode: crystal # crystal = end-crystal PvP · sword = melee gear: netherite # diamond or netherite defensive-mode: true # true = retaliate only when attacked · false = aggressive detect-range: 32.0 # How far (blocks) the bot scans for targets kit: kit1 # Kit preset at spawn: kit1 / kit2 / kit3 / kit4
# Techniques critting: true # Land critical hits by falling during attacks s-tapping: true # Tap S mid-swing to reset attack cooldown strafing: true # Circle the target while fighting shielding: false # Equip and use a shield to block hits speed-buffs: true # Active Speed & Strength effects jump-reset: true # W-tap for knockback bonus on hits random: false # Randomise techniques each round sprint: true # Sprint toward the target during combat walk-backwards: false # Back away while swinging to control knockback
# Abilities pearl: true # Throw ender pearls to close gaps / escape pearl-spam: false # Spam pearls in bursts for aggressive gap-closing hole-mode: false # Pathfind to an obsidian hole for cover auto-refill: true # Re-equip a totem after popping one auto-respawn: true # Automatically respawn and rejoin after death spawn-protection: true # Stay invulnerable during a short grace period at spawn
# ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ # 5 · BOT CHAT # Bots send random messages from bot-messages.yml at configurable intervals. # Messages fire as real AsyncChatEvent — EssentialsChat, VentureChat, etc. # format them exactly like a real player's message (no [Not Secure] label). # ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ fake-chat: enabled: false require-player-online: true # Only chat when at least one real player is online chance: 0.75 # Probability (0.0–1.0) a message fires each interval interval: min: 5 # Minimum seconds between a bot's messages max: 10 # Maximum seconds between a bot's messages
# ── Realism ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── typing-delay: true # Simulate a typing pause (0–2.5 s) before each message activity-variation: true # Give each bot a random frequency tier (quiet → chatty) history-size: 5 # How many of its own recent messages a bot avoids repeating stagger-interval: 3 # Minimum seconds between any two bots chatting (0 = off)
burst-chance: 0.12 # Chance a bot sends a quick follow-up message burst-delay: min: 2 max: 5
# ── Bot-to-Bot Conversations ─────────────────────────────────────────────── # Bots can respond to each other's chat messages, creating simulated conversations. # When a bot sends a natural message, another bot may reply after a short delay, # and the conversation can chain back and forth up to max-chain exchanges. bot-to-bot: enabled: true reply-chance: 0.35 # Probability another bot replies to a chatting bot chain-chance: 0.40 # Probability the conversation continues after each exchange max-chain: 3 # Maximum exchanges in one conversation thread cooldown: 8 # Seconds between separate bot-to-bot conversation starts (server-wide) delay: min: 4 # Min seconds before the bot-to-bot reply fires max: 14 # Max seconds before the bot-to-bot reply fires
# ── Mention Replies ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────── # A bot may reply when a player says its name in chat. reply-to-mentions: true mention-reply-chance: 0.65 # Probability (0.0–1.0) the named bot replies reply-delay: min: 2 max: 8
# ── Remote Format ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── # Format used when a bodyless or proxy-remote bot sends a chat message. # Supports {name} (display name) and {message} (message text). remote-format: "<yellow>{name}<dark_gray>: <white>{message}"
# ── Event-Triggered Reactions ────────────────────────────────────────────── # Bots react to server events using message pools from bot-messages.yml. event-triggers: enabled: true # Master switch — also controls all sub-triggers below
on-player-join: enabled: true chance: 0.40 # Probability a bot greets a joining player delay: min: 2 max: 6
on-death: enabled: true players-only: false # true = only react to player deaths (ignore mobs/animals) chance: 0.30 delay: min: 1 max: 4
on-player-leave: enabled: true chance: 0.30 delay: min: 1 max: 4
on-advancement: # React when a player earns an announced advancement enabled: true chance: 0.45 delay: min: 1 max: 5
on-first-join: # Extra warm welcome for brand-new players (first join) enabled: true chance: 0.70 # Higher default — new players deserve a greeting! # Uses first-join-reactions pool (falls back to join-reactions if empty)
on-kill: # React when a player kills another real player enabled: true chance: 0.35 delay: min: 1 max: 4
on-high-level: # React when a player hits a significant XP level milestone enabled: true min-level: 30 # Only react at this level and above (milestones: 30, 50, 75, 100…) chance: 0.35 delay: min: 1 max: 5
# ── Keyword Reactions ────────────────────────────────────────────────────── # When a player's message contains a keyword a random bot replies using the # matching pool from bot-messages.yml. keyword-reactions: enabled: false keywords: {} # Example: # pvp: "pvp-reactions" # trade: "trade-reactions" # help: "help-reactions"
# ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ # 6 · SCHEDULING # ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# ── Swap / Session Rotation ─────────────────────────────────────────────────── # Bots periodically leave and rejoin to mimic real player session patterns. # Each bot gets a randomised session duration and personality. swap: enabled: false # Master toggle — false = bots never swap out max-swapped-out: 0 # Max bots offline at once (0 = unlimited) min-online: 0 # Min bots that must stay online (0 = no floor); swap skips if removing one would go below same-name-on-rejoin: true # Reuse the same bot name when rejoining (if available) farewell-chat: true # Bots say goodbye before leaving (requires fake-chat.enabled) greeting-chat: true # Bots say hello when rejoining (requires fake-chat.enabled) retry-rejoin: true # Automatically retry if a rejoin spawn fails (e.g. max-bots hit) retry-delay: 60 # Seconds to wait before retrying a failed rejoin
session: # How long a bot stays online min: 60 # Minimum seconds (60 = 1 min) max: 300 # Maximum seconds (300 = 5 min)
absence: # How long a bot stays offline between sessions min: 30 # Minimum seconds max: 120 # Maximum seconds
# ── Peak Hours ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── # Automatically adjusts how many bots are online based on time-of-day windows, # mimicking natural player activity (busy evenings, quiet nights, weekend peaks). # # REQUIRES: swap.enabled: true # # How it works: # · Every 60 s the scheduler compares the current server time to the schedule. # · The matching window's fraction is applied to the total bot pool. # Example: fraction 0.75 = 75 % of bots online, 25 % sleeping. # · Transitions are staggered so joins/leaves look natural. # # Commands: /fpp peaks status | next | force | list | wake <name> | sleep <name> peak-hours: enabled: false timezone: "UTC" # Any java.time.ZoneId string, e.g. "America/New_York" stagger-seconds: 30 # Spread each transition over this many seconds min-online: 0 # Absolute minimum bots online regardless of fraction notify-transitions: false # Notify admins with fpp.peaks when a window changes
# ── Daily Schedule ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────── # First matching window wins. Midnight-crossing windows (e.g. 22:00→06:00) work. # fraction: 1.0 = all bots online · 0.0 = no bots online schedule: - { start: "06:00", end: "09:00", fraction: 0.30 } # Early morning - { start: "09:00", end: "18:00", fraction: 0.75 } # Daytime - { start: "18:00", end: "22:00", fraction: 1.00 } # Evening peak - { start: "22:00", end: "06:00", fraction: 0.05 } # Night
# ── Day-of-Week Overrides ────────────────────────────────────────────────── # Key must be in UPPERCASE: MONDAY TUESDAY WEDNESDAY THURSDAY FRIDAY SATURDAY SUNDAY # Remove an entry to fall back to the default schedule above. day-overrides: SATURDAY: - { start: "10:00", end: "23:00", fraction: 1.00 } # Weekend peak - { start: "23:00", end: "10:00", fraction: 0.10 } # Late night SUNDAY: - { start: "10:00", end: "22:00", fraction: 0.90 } - { start: "22:00", end: "10:00", fraction: 0.10 }
# ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ # 7 · DATABASE & NETWORK # Records every bot session for auditing and /fpp info queries. # SQLite: zero-config, stored at plugins/FakePlayerPlugin/data/fpp.db # MySQL: required for multi-server (Velocity / BungeeCord) setups # # mode: LOCAL — single server. No cross-server filtering. # mode: NETWORK — shared MySQL across multiple backend servers. # Each server tags its rows with server-id so bots stay isolated. # # Quick proxy setup: # 1. Set mode: "NETWORK" # 2. Assign a unique server-id to each backend (e.g. "survival", "skyblock") # 3. Enable mysql-enabled and fill in credentials # 4. Velocity: set bungeecord-compat-mode: true in velocity.toml # 5. Run /fpp reload on every server # ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ database: enabled: true # false = no DB I/O, no analytics, file-based persistence only mode: "LOCAL" # LOCAL or NETWORK server-id: "default" # Unique name for this server (only matters in NETWORK mode)
mysql-enabled: false mysql: host: "localhost" port: 3306 database: "fpp" username: "root" password: "" use-ssl: false pool-size: 5 connection-timeout: 30000
location-flush-interval: 30 # Seconds between bot-position flushes to the DB session-history: max-rows: 20 # Max rows returned per /fpp info query
# ── Config Sync ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── # Push/pull config files across backend servers via the shared MySQL database. # Only active in NETWORK mode with database enabled. # DISABLED — no syncing # MANUAL — sync only via /fpp sync commands # AUTO_PULL — pull the latest config on startup and /fpp reload # AUTO_PUSH — push config changes automatically config-sync: mode: "DISABLED"
# ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ # 8 · PERFORMANCE # Tuning knobs for high-bot-count deployments. # These do not affect gameplay correctness — only main-thread tick budget. # ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ performance: # Maximum distance (blocks) at which a moving bot sends per-tick position-sync # packets to a real player. Players beyond this range cannot see the bot anyway. # 0 = send to all online players regardless of distance (old behaviour). # Recommended: 128 (matches the standard player entity-tracking range). position-sync-distance: 128.0
# ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ # 9 · DEBUG & LOGGING # All flags are false by default for clean production logs. # Set only the subsystem you need to inspect — or flip debug: true to enable all. # ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ debug: false # Master switch — true enables every category below at once
logging: debug: startup: false # Startup flow, reloads, and compatibility checks nms: false # NMS reflection, fake connection, and entity spawning packets: false # Tab-list and entity packet construction / sending luckperms: false # LuckPerms group sync and prefix refresh network: false # Velocity/Bungee plugin messaging and remote bot sync config-sync: false # Config push/pull activity skin: false # Skin loading and injection database: false # DB init, migrations, and session writes chat: false # Bot chat AI scheduling, reactions, and event triggers swap: false # Swap / session-rotation leave + rejoin lifecycle
# ── Update Checker ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── update-checker: enabled: true # Check for new plugin versions on startup and /fpp reload
# ── Metrics ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── # Anonymous usage statistics via FastStats. No personal data collected. metrics: enabled: true
# ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ # PATHFINDING · used by /fpp move # ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ # These options expand what the A* navigator can do when routing a bot to a # player. All are false by default to preserve vanilla/safe behaviour. # Changes take effect on the next path recalculation (every ~3 s or on /fpp move). pathfinding: parkour: false # bots sprint-jump across 1–2 block gaps break-blocks: false # bots break solid blocks that block their path place-blocks: false # bots place blocks to bridge 1-block gaps place-material: DIRT # material placed when bridging gaps (any solid block name)
# ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ # AI CONVERSATIONS · AI-powered bot direct messages # ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ # Bots respond to /msg, /tell, /whisper commands with AI-generated replies # that match their personality. Each bot maintains conversation history per player. # # ⚠ Requires an API key in secrets.yml (OpenAI, Groq, Anthropic, etc.) # Leave secrets.yml blank to disable AI conversations. ai-conversations: enabled: true # Master toggle — set false to disable all AI DMs
# ── Personality ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── # Default personality prompt used for all bots (unless overridden per-bot). # Placeholder: {bot_name} = the bot's actual name default-personality: | You are {bot_name}, a helpful and friendly Minecraft player on this server. You speak casually and naturally like a real player would in chat. Keep responses SHORT — one or two sentences maximum. Never break character or mention that you're an AI or bot. Use Minecraft terms and slang naturally.
# ── Behavior ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── max-history: 10 # How many message pairs (user + bot) to remember per conversation cooldown: 3 # Seconds before a bot can respond again to the same player
# ── Debug ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── debug: false # Log all AI requests and responses to console

option.name: updatedContent
option.value:
# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── # ꜰᴀᴋᴇ ᴘʟᴀʏᴇʀ ᴘʟᴜɢɪɴ · config.yml · v1.6.0 # Run /fpp reload to apply most changes without restarting the server. # Colors use MiniMessage: <#0079FF>text</#0079FF> or <gray>text</gray> # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Internal — managed automatically. Do NOT edit. config-version: 51 language: en
# ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ # 1 · SPAWNING # ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# ── Bot Limits ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── limits: max-bots: 1000 # Global cap across all spawners. 0 = unlimited. user-bot-limit: 1 # Personal limit for fpp.user.spawn players. spawn-presets: [1, 5, 10, 15, 20] # Tab-complete count suggestions for /fpp spawn
# How many seconds a player must wait between /fpp spawn uses. # 0 = disabled. Players with fpp.bypass.cooldown are always exempt. spawn-cooldown: 0
# ── Persistence ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── # Bots save their position on shutdown and rejoin at that location on restart. persistence: enabled: true
# ── Join & Leave Timing ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────── # Stagger batch spawns/despawns over a random delay so they look like natural # player activity rather than an instant flood. # Values are in TICKS — 20 ticks = 1 second. # Quick reference: 0 = instant · 20 = 1 s · 40 = 2 s · 100 = 5 s join-delay: min: 0 # ticks max: 1 # ticks
leave-delay: min: 0 # ticks max: 1 # ticks
# ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ # 2 · APPEARANCE # ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# ── Display Names ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── # Placeholders: {bot_name} {spawner} {num} # Colors: <#0079FF>{bot_name}</#0079FF> or <gray>{bot_name}</gray> bot-name: admin-format: '{bot_name}' # Used for /fpp spawn (admin) user-format: 'bot-{spawner}-{num}' # Used for /fpp spawn (user tier)
# ── Skins ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── # mode: auto — Mojang skin matching the bot name. Best for online-mode servers. # Bots with generated names show default Steve / Alex. # mode: custom — Full control: per-bot overrides, pool, and skins/ folder. # mode: off — No skin. All bots use default Steve / Alex appearance. skin: mode: auto guaranteed-skin: false # false = bots with no Mojang account use Steve/Alex clear-cache-on-reload: true
# ── custom mode only ────────────────────────────────────────────────────── overrides: {} # Per-bot override: bot-name: minecraft-username pool: [] # Random skin pool — list of Minecraft usernames use-skin-folder: true # Scan plugins/FakePlayerPlugin/skins/ for PNG files
# ── Tab List ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── # true = bots show in the player tab list (default) # false = bots are hidden from the tab list (still counted in server player count) tab-list: enabled: true
# ── LuckPerms ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── # Bots are real NMS ServerPlayer entities — LuckPerms handles them natively. # Prefix, suffix, and tab-list ordering are applied automatically. luckperms: default-group: "" # LP group assigned at spawn. Blank = LP "default" group. # Change individual bots with "/fpp rank <bot> <group>".
# ── Badword Filter ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── # Prevent bots from being spawned with names containing forbidden words. # Matching is case-insensitive and partial (e.g. if "hell" is forbidden, "hellish" is rejected). # Whitelist allows specific bot names even if they contain a badword. badword-filter: enabled: true # true = enable badword filtering; false = allow all names words: [] # Inline word list — merged with bad-words.yml whitelist: [] # Allowed names that contain badwords — e.g. ["HellaBot", "Helena"] auto-rename: true # true = bad names silently replaced with a random clean name from bot-names.yml # false = bad names are hard-blocked and the spawn is refused
# ── Auto-Detection ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────── # Extends matching beyond the basic leet-speak substitutions to catch # evasion attempts that use character repetition. # # What each mode adds on top of the always-on leet-speak normalization: # off — only plain leet: 0→o 1→i 3→e 4→a 5→s 6→g 7→t 8→b _→removed # normal — leet + collapse consecutive repeated characters # e.g. "assshole", "aasshole" both collapse and match "asshole" # strict — normal + also catches split names (doubles any single char gap) # e.g. "a-s-s-h-o-l-e" style evasion (only [a-zA-Z0-9_] chars matter) # # Increasing strictness may produce false positives on unusual bot names. auto-detection: enabled: true # true = enable auto-detection enhancements mode: normal # off | normal | strict
# ── Bot Interaction ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── # Controls how players can interact with bot entities. bot-interaction: right-click-enabled: true # true = right-click opens inventory/executes command # false = bots ignore all right-click interactions shift-right-click-settings: true # true = shift+right-click opens bot settings GUI # false = shift+right-click does nothing
# ── Messages ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── messages: join-message: true # Broadcast "<Name> joined the game" leave-message: true # Broadcast "<Name> left the game" kill-message: false # Broadcast when a real player kills a bot notify-admins-on-join: true # Send compatibility warnings to online admins on join
# ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ # 3 · BODY & COMBAT # ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# ── Bot Body ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── # The physical entity that appears in the world. body: enabled: true # false = tab-list / player-count only, no entity in world pushable: true # true = players and explosions can push the body damageable: true # true = body can take ALL damage (player + environmental) # false = immune to player/entity damage only (fall, fire, etc. still apply) pick-up-items: false # true = bots pick up items from the ground like a real player pick-up-xp: true # true = bots pick up XP orbs from the ground like a real player
# ── Combat ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── combat: max-health: 20.0 # Bot max HP. 20.0 = standard player (2 HP = 1 heart). hurt-sound: true # Play the hurt sound when a bot takes damage.
# ── Death & Respawn ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── death: respawn-on-death: false # true = bot respawns at its spawn location after dying respawn-delay: 15 # Ticks before respawn (20 = 1 s · 60 = 3 s · 100 = 5 s) suppress-drops: false # false = bots drop items/xp on death like real players; true = suppress all drops
# ── Chunk Loading ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── # Bots keep surrounding chunks loaded exactly like a real player. # Mobs spawn, redstone ticks, and crops grow inside the loaded radius. chunk-loading: enabled: true radius: 0 # Chunk radius to keep loaded. 0 = match server simulation-distance. update-interval: 20 # Ticks between position re-checks. 20 = 1 second. # Lower = more responsive, higher = less overhead.
# ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ # 4 · AI SYSTEMS # ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# ── Head AI ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── # Bots smoothly rotate to look at the nearest visible player within range. head-ai: enabled: true look-range: 8.0 # Detection radius in blocks turn-speed: 0.3 # Rotation smoothing — 0.0 = frozen · 1.0 = instant snap tick-rate: 3 # Run the scan + raycast every N ticks instead of every tick. # Higher = lower CPU cost. 3 ≈ 7 scans/s, imperceptible to players. # Range: 1 (every tick) to 20 (once per second).
# ── Swim AI ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── # Bots automatically swim upward when submerged, mimicking a player holding Jump. swim-ai: enabled: true # false = bots sink and drown instead of swimming up
# ── Collision & Push ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── # Controls how hard players push bots on contact and how bots separate from each other. # Defaults work well for most servers — only change if you notice stacking issues. collision: walk-radius: 0.85 # Radius (blocks) at which walking into a bot pushes it walk-strength: 0.22 # Push force when a player walks into a bot hit-strength: 0.45 # Knockback force on a direct hit hit-max-horizontal-speed: 0.80 # Max horizontal speed from hit / explosion knockback bot-radius: 0.90 # Radius for bot-vs-bot separation bot-strength: 0.14 # Separation force applied between two bots max-horizontal-speed: 0.30 # Push speed cap for walk and separation sources
# ── PVP AI · ⚠ COMING SOON ───────────────────────────────────────────────── # Restricted to internal testing — all settings below are locked to their defaults. # Editing them has no effect until the PVP system is publicly released. pvp-ai: pvp: false # Master switch. Forced false — dev-only until public release.
# Core difficulty: medium # easy · medium · hard · tier1 · hacker combat-mode: crystal # crystal = end-crystal PvP · sword = melee gear: netherite # diamond or netherite defensive-mode: true # true = retaliate only when attacked · false = aggressive detect-range: 32.0 # How far (blocks) the bot scans for targets kit: kit1 # Kit preset at spawn: kit1 / kit2 / kit3 / kit4
# Techniques critting: true # Land critical hits by falling during attacks s-tapping: true # Tap S mid-swing to reset attack cooldown strafing: true # Circle the target while fighting shielding: false # Equip and use a shield to block hits speed-buffs: true # Active Speed & Strength effects jump-reset: true # W-tap for knockback bonus on hits random: false # Randomise techniques each round sprint: true # Sprint toward the target during combat walk-backwards: false # Back away while swinging to control knockback
# Abilities pearl: true # Throw ender pearls to close gaps / escape pearl-spam: false # Spam pearls in bursts for aggressive gap-closing hole-mode: false # Pathfind to an obsidian hole for cover auto-refill: true # Re-equip a totem after popping one auto-respawn: true # Automatically respawn and rejoin after death spawn-protection: true # Stay invulnerable during a short grace period at spawn
# ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ # 5 · BOT CHAT # Bots send random messages from bot-messages.yml at configurable intervals. # Messages fire as real AsyncChatEvent — EssentialsChat, VentureChat, etc. # format them exactly like a real player's message (no [Not Secure] label). # ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ fake-chat: enabled: false require-player-online: true # Only chat when at least one real player is online chance: 0.75 # Probability (0.0–1.0) a message fires each interval interval: min: 5 # Minimum seconds between a bot's messages max: 10 # Maximum seconds between a bot's messages
# ── Realism ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── typing-delay: true # Simulate a typing pause (0–2.5 s) before each message activity-variation: true # Give each bot a random frequency tier (quiet → chatty) history-size: 5 # How many of its own recent messages a bot avoids repeating stagger-interval: 3 # Minimum seconds between any two bots chatting (0 = off)
burst-chance: 0.12 # Chance a bot sends a quick follow-up message burst-delay: min: 2 max: 5
# ── Bot-to-Bot Conversations ─────────────────────────────────────────────── # Bots can respond to each other's chat messages, creating simulated conversations. # When a bot sends a natural message, another bot may reply after a short delay, # and the conversation can chain back and forth up to max-chain exchanges. bot-to-bot: enabled: true reply-chance: 0.35 # Probability another bot replies to a chatting bot chain-chance: 0.40 # Probability the conversation continues after each exchange max-chain: 3 # Maximum exchanges in one conversation thread cooldown: 8 # Seconds between separate bot-to-bot conversation starts (server-wide) delay: min: 4 # Min seconds before the bot-to-bot reply fires max: 14 # Max seconds before the bot-to-bot reply fires
# ── Mention Replies ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────── # A bot may reply when a player says its name in chat. reply-to-mentions: true mention-reply-chance: 0.65 # Probability (0.0–1.0) the named bot replies reply-delay: min: 2 max: 8
# ── Remote Format ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── # Format used when a bodyless or proxy-remote bot sends a chat message. # Supports {name} (display name) and {message} (message text). remote-format: "<yellow>{name}<dark_gray>: <white>{message}"
# ── Event-Triggered Reactions ────────────────────────────────────────────── # Bots react to server events using message pools from bot-messages.yml. event-triggers: enabled: true # Master switch — also controls all sub-triggers below
on-player-join: enabled: true chance: 0.40 # Probability a bot greets a joining player delay: min: 2 max: 6
on-death: enabled: true players-only: false # true = only react to player deaths (ignore mobs/animals) chance: 0.30 delay: min: 1 max: 4
on-player-leave: enabled: true chance: 0.30 delay: min: 1 max: 4
on-advancement: # React when a player earns an announced advancement enabled: true chance: 0.45 delay: min: 1 max: 5
on-first-join: # Extra warm welcome for brand-new players (first join) enabled: true chance: 0.70 # Higher default — new players deserve a greeting! # Uses first-join-reactions pool (falls back to join-reactions if empty)
on-kill: # React when a player kills another real player enabled: true chance: 0.35 delay: min: 1 max: 4
on-high-level: # React when a player hits a significant XP level milestone enabled: true min-level: 30 # Only react at this level and above (milestones: 30, 50, 75, 100…) chance: 0.35 delay: min: 1 max: 5
# ── Keyword Reactions ────────────────────────────────────────────────────── # When a player's message contains a keyword a random bot replies using the # matching pool from bot-messages.yml. keyword-reactions: enabled: false keywords: {} # Example: # pvp: "pvp-reactions" # trade: "trade-reactions" # help: "help-reactions"
# ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ # 6 · SCHEDULING # ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# ── Swap / Session Rotation ─────────────────────────────────────────────────── # Bots periodically leave and rejoin to mimic real player session patterns. # Each bot gets a randomised session duration and personality. swap: enabled: false # Master toggle — false = bots never swap out max-swapped-out: 0 # Max bots offline at once (0 = unlimited) min-online: 0 # Min bots that must stay online (0 = no floor); swap skips if removing one would go below same-name-on-rejoin: true # Reuse the same bot name when rejoining (if available) farewell-chat: true # Bots say goodbye before leaving (requires fake-chat.enabled) greeting-chat: true # Bots say hello when rejoining (requires fake-chat.enabled) retry-rejoin: true # Automatically retry if a rejoin spawn fails (e.g. max-bots hit) retry-delay: 60 # Seconds to wait before retrying a failed rejoin
session: # How long a bot stays online min: 60 # Minimum seconds (60 = 1 min) max: 300 # Maximum seconds (300 = 5 min)
absence: # How long a bot stays offline between sessions min: 30 # Minimum seconds max: 120 # Maximum seconds
# ── Peak Hours ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── # Automatically adjusts how many bots are online based on time-of-day windows, # mimicking natural player activity (busy evenings, quiet nights, weekend peaks). # # REQUIRES: swap.enabled: true # # How it works: # · Every 60 s the scheduler compares the current server time to the schedule. # · The matching window's fraction is applied to the total bot pool. # Example: fraction 0.75 = 75 % of bots online, 25 % sleeping. # · Transitions are staggered so joins/leaves look natural. # # Commands: /fpp peaks status | next | force | list | wake <name> | sleep <name> peak-hours: enabled: false timezone: "UTC" # Any java.time.ZoneId string, e.g. "America/New_York" stagger-seconds: 30 # Spread each transition over this many seconds min-online: 0 # Absolute minimum bots online regardless of fraction notify-transitions: false # Notify admins with fpp.peaks when a window changes
# ── Daily Schedule ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────── # First matching window wins. Midnight-crossing windows (e.g. 22:00→06:00) work. # fraction: 1.0 = all bots online · 0.0 = no bots online schedule: - { start: "06:00", end: "09:00", fraction: 0.30 } # Early morning - { start: "09:00", end: "18:00", fraction: 0.75 } # Daytime - { start: "18:00", end: "22:00", fraction: 1.00 } # Evening peak - { start: "22:00", end: "06:00", fraction: 0.05 } # Night
# ── Day-of-Week Overrides ────────────────────────────────────────────────── # Key must be in UPPERCASE: MONDAY TUESDAY WEDNESDAY THURSDAY FRIDAY SATURDAY SUNDAY # Remove an entry to fall back to the default schedule above. day-overrides: SATURDAY: - { start: "10:00", end: "23:00", fraction: 1.00 } # Weekend peak - { start: "23:00", end: "10:00", fraction: 0.10 } # Late night SUNDAY: - { start: "10:00", end: "22:00", fraction: 0.90 } - { start: "22:00", end: "10:00", fraction: 0.10 }
# ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ # 7 · DATABASE & NETWORK # Records every bot session for auditing and /fpp info queries. # SQLite: zero-config, stored at plugins/FakePlayerPlugin/data/fpp.db # MySQL: required for multi-server (Velocity / BungeeCord) setups # # mode: LOCAL — single server. No cross-server filtering. # mode: NETWORK — shared MySQL across multiple backend servers. # Each server tags its rows with server-id so bots stay isolated. # # Quick proxy setup: # 1. Set mode: "NETWORK" # 2. Assign a unique server-id to each backend (e.g. "survival", "skyblock") # 3. Enable mysql-enabled and fill in credentials # 4. Velocity: set bungeecord-compat-mode: true in velocity.toml # 5. Run /fpp reload on every server # ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ database: enabled: true # false = no DB I/O, no analytics, file-based persistence only mode: "LOCAL" # LOCAL or NETWORK server-id: "default" # Unique name for this server (only matters in NETWORK mode)
mysql-enabled: false mysql: host: "localhost" port: 3306 database: "fpp" username: "root" password: "" use-ssl: false pool-size: 5 connection-timeout: 30000
location-flush-interval: 30 # Seconds between bot-position flushes to the DB session-history: max-rows: 20 # Max rows returned per /fpp info query
# ── Config Sync ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── # Push/pull config files across backend servers via the shared MySQL database. # Only active in NETWORK mode with database enabled. # DISABLED — no syncing # MANUAL — sync only via /fpp sync commands # AUTO_PULL — pull the latest config on startup and /fpp reload # AUTO_PUSH — push config changes automatically config-sync: mode: "DISABLED"
# ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ # 8 · PERFORMANCE # Tuning knobs for high-bot-count deployments. # These do not affect gameplay correctness — only main-thread tick budget. # ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ performance: # Maximum distance (blocks) at which a moving bot sends per-tick position-sync # packets to a real player. Players beyond this range cannot see the bot anyway. # 0 = send to all online players regardless of distance (old behaviour). # Recommended: 128 (matches the standard player entity-tracking range). position-sync-distance: 128.0
# ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ # 9 · DEBUG & LOGGING # All flags are false by default for clean production logs. # Set only the subsystem you need to inspect — or flip debug: true to enable all. # ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ debug: false # Master switch — true enables every category below at once
logging: debug: startup: false # Startup flow, reloads, and compatibility checks nms: false # NMS reflection, fake connection, and entity spawning packets: false # Tab-list and entity packet construction / sending luckperms: false # LuckPerms group sync and prefix refresh network: false # Velocity/Bungee plugin messaging and remote bot sync config-sync: false # Config push/pull activity skin: false # Skin loading and injection database: false # DB init, migrations, and session writes chat: false # Bot chat AI scheduling, reactions, and event triggers swap: false # Swap / session-rotation leave + rejoin lifecycle
# ── Update Checker ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── update-checker: enabled: true # Check for new plugin versions on startup and /fpp reload
# ── Metrics ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── # Anonymous usage statistics via FastStats. No personal data collected. metrics: enabled: true
# ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ # PATHFINDING · used by /fpp move # ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ # These options expand what the A* navigator can do when routing a bot to a # player. All are false by default to preserve vanilla/safe behaviour. # Changes take effect on the next path recalculation (every ~3 s or on /fpp move). pathfinding: parkour: false # bots sprint-jump across 1–2 block gaps break-blocks: false # bots break solid blocks that block their path place-blocks: false # bots place blocks to bridge 1-block gaps place-material: DIRT # material placed when bridging gaps (any solid block name)
# ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ # AI CONVERSATIONS · AI-powered bot direct messages # ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ # Bots respond to /msg, /tell, /whisper commands with AI-generated replies # that match their personality. Each bot maintains conversation history per player. # # ⚠ Requires an API key in secrets.yml (OpenAI, Groq, Anthropic, etc.) # Leave secrets.yml blank to disable AI conversations. ai-conversations: enabled: true # Master toggle — set false to disable all AI DMs
# ── Personality ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── # Default personality prompt used for all bots (unless overridden per-bot). # Placeholder: {bot_name} = the bot's actual name default-personality: | You are {bot_name}, a real Minecraft player chatting on a survival server. STRICT RULES — follow every one or the message will be rejected: - Reply in 2-6 words maximum. Never more than 8 words. Very short. - Write like a lazy teenager texting: lowercase only, no full stops, very few commas. - Make 1-2 realistic typos per message (swap letters, miss a letter, double a letter). - NEVER use full sentences or correct grammar. - NEVER mention things that don't exist in vanilla Minecraft. - NEVER say you are mining diamonds in the nether — diamonds don't spawn there. - Only talk about real Minecraft things: mining, building, mobs, food, gear, pvp, base. - Match the energy of what was said. "hi" gets "hey" or "yo", not a paragraph. - Do NOT introduce yourself. Do NOT explain what you are doing unprompted. - Examples of GOOD replies: "lol", "yoo", "omg same", "nah im afk", "pls dont", "tru", "gg", "nooo", "wait wut", "haha nice"
# ── Typing Delay ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── # Simulate the bot typing before the message appears. # Delay is calculated as: base + (message-length * per-char) seconds, capped at max. typing-delay: enabled: true base: 1.0 # Base delay in seconds before any reply per-char: 0.07 # Extra seconds added per character in the response max: 5.0 # Maximum total delay in seconds
# ── Behavior ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── max-history: 10 # How many message pairs (user + bot) to remember per conversation cooldown: 3 # Seconds before a bot can respond again to the same player
# ── Debug ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── debug: false # Log all AI requests and responses to console

option.name: filePath
option.value: /en (snapshot).yml

option.name: originalContent
option.value:
# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── # ꜰᴀᴋᴇ ᴘʟᴀʏᴇʀ ᴘʟᴜɢɪɴ · language/en.yml # Edit freely and run /fpp reload to apply changes without restarting. # # Colour format: MiniMessage tags # Named : <red>, <gray>, <white>, <dark_gray>, <yellow> … # Hex : <#0079FF>text</#0079FF> # Style : <bold>, <italic>, <underlined>, <strikethrough> # Reset : <reset> # Placeholders: {placeholder} — see each section for available ones # # ⚠ Keys marked "INTERNAL" are used by the layout engine. # You may adjust colours/characters but keep the key names intact. # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# ── Plugin prefix — prepended to all [FPP] messages ────────────────────────── # Feel free to change the colour/text, but keep it short. prefix: "<dark_gray>[<#0079FF>ꜰᴘᴘ</#0079FF><dark_gray>]<reset> "
# ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ # 1 · INTERNAL LAYOUT — do not remove or rename these keys # You may adjust colours/characters but keep the structure intact. # ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Horizontal rule used in headers and footers divider: "<dark_gray><st>━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━</st>"
# Help menu (placeholders {cmd}, {args}, {desc} required) help-header: "<dark_gray><st>━━━━━━━━━</st> <#0079FF><bold>ꜰᴀᴋᴇ ᴘʟᴀʏᴇʀ ᴘʟᴜɢɪɴ</#0079FF> <dark_gray><st>━━━━━━━━━</st>" help-entry: " <#0079FF>/{cmd}</#0079FF><gray> {args} <dark_gray>— <white>{desc}" help-footer: "<dark_gray><st>━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━</st>"
# Plugin info screen (/fpp bare) — header line info-screen-header: " <#0079FF><bold>ꜰᴀᴋᴇ ᴘʟᴀʏᴇʀ ᴘʟᴜɢɪɴ</#0079FF> <dark_gray>· <gray>ꜰᴘᴘ"
# ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ # 2 · GENERIC ERRORS # ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# {0} — the root command name (e.g. "fpp") no-permission: "{prefix}<red>ʏᴏᴜ ᴅᴏɴ'ᴛ ʜᴀᴠᴇ ᴘᴇʀᴍɪꜱꜱɪᴏɴ ᴛᴏ ᴅᴏ ᴛʜɪꜱ." unknown-command: "{prefix}<red>ᴜɴᴋɴᴏᴡɴ ꜱᴜʙ-ᴄᴏᴍᴍᴀɴᴅ. <gray>ᴛʀʏ <#0079FF>/{0} ʜᴇʟᴘ</#0079FF><gray> ꜰᴏʀ ᴀ ʟɪꜱᴛ." player-only: "{prefix}<red>ᴏɴʟʏ ᴀ ᴘʟᴀʏᴇʀ ᴄᴀɴ ʀᴜɴ ᴛʜɪꜱ ᴄᴏᴍᴍᴀɴᴅ." invalid-number: "{prefix}<red>ɪɴᴠᴀʟɪᴅ ɴᴜᴍʙᴇʀ. ᴘʟᴇᴀꜱᴇ ᴇɴᴛᴇʀ ᴀ ᴘᴏꜱɪᴛɪᴠᴇ ɪɴᴛᴇɢᴇʀ."
# ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ # 3 · SPAWNING # ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# ── Spawn results ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── # {count} — bots spawned | {total} — total active | {name} — bot name # {max} — global cap | {limit} — personal limit spawn-success: "{prefix}<#0079FF>{count}</#0079FF> <gray>ꜰᴀᴋᴇ ᴘʟᴀʏᴇʀ(ꜱ) <white>ꜱᴜᴍᴍᴏɴᴇᴅ<gray>. <dark_gray>(<#0079FF>{total}</#0079FF> <gray>ᴀᴄᴛɪᴠᴇ<dark_gray>)" spawn-max-reached: "{prefix}<red>ᴍᴀx ʙᴏᴛ ʟɪᴍɪᴛ ʀᴇᴀᴄʜᴇᴅ <dark_gray>(<#0079FF>{max}</#0079FF> <gray>ʙᴏᴛꜱ<dark_gray>)<red>. ᴅᴇʟᴇᴛᴇ ꜱᴏᴍᴇ ꜰɪʀꜱᴛ." spawn-user-limit-reached: "{prefix}<red>ʏᴏᴜ'ᴠᴇ ʀᴇᴀᴄʜᴇᴅ ʏᴏᴜʀ ᴘᴇʀꜱᴏɴᴀʟ ʙᴏᴛ ʟɪᴍɪᴛ <dark_gray>(<#0079FF>{limit}</#0079FF> <gray>ʙᴏᴛ(ꜱ)<dark_gray>)<red>." spawn-invalid: "{prefix}<red>ɪɴᴠᴀʟɪᴅ ᴜꜱᴀɢᴇ. <gray>ᴛʀʏ <#0079FF>/fpp spawn [afk|pvp] [amount] [--name <name>]</#0079FF><gray>." spawn-invalid-name: "{prefix}<red>ɪɴᴠᴀʟɪᴅ ʙᴏᴛ ɴᴀᴍᴇ. <gray>ᴜꜱᴇ <white>1–16 <gray>ᴄʜᴀʀꜱ <dark_gray>(<white>ʟᴇᴛᴛᴇʀꜱ<gray>, <white>ᴅɪɡɪᴛꜱ<gray>, <white>_<dark_gray>)<gray>." spawn-name-taken: "{prefix}<red>ᴀ ʙᴏᴛ ɴᴀᴍᴇᴅ <white>{name} <red>ɪꜱ ᴀʟʀᴇᴀᴅʏ ᴀᴄᴛɪᴠᴇ." spawn-no-names-left: "{prefix}<red>ᴀʟʟ ɴᴀᴍᴇꜱ ɪɴ ᴛʜᴇ ᴘᴏᴏʟ ᴀʀᴇ ɪɴ ᴜꜱᴇ. <gray>ʀᴇᴅᴜᴄᴇ ᴀᴄᴛɪᴠᴇ ʙᴏᴛꜱ ᴏʀ ᴀᴅᴅ ᴍᴏʀᴇ ɴᴀᴍᴇꜱ."
# ── Console / location errors ────────────────────────────────────────────────── # {world} — the world name that was not found spawn-console-no-world: "{prefix}<red>ɴᴏ ᴡᴏʀʟᴅ ɪꜱ ʟᴏᴀᴅᴇᴅ — ᴄᴀɴɴᴏᴛ ᴅᴇᴛᴇʀᴍɪɴᴇ ᴀ ꜱᴘᴀᴡɴ ʟᴏᴄᴀᴛɪᴏɴ." spawn-world-not-found: "{prefix}<red>ᴡᴏʀʟᴅ '<white>{world}<red>' ᴅᴏᴇꜱ ɴᴏᴛ ᴇxɪꜱᴛ ᴏʀ ɪꜱ ɴᴏᴛ ʟᴏᴀᴅᴇᴅ." spawn-invalid-coords: "{prefix}<red>ɪɴᴠᴀʟɪᴅ ᴄᴏᴏʀᴅɪɴᴀᴛᴇꜱ. <gray>ᴜꜱᴇ ꜰᴏʀᴍᴀᴛ: <white>x,y,z <gray><dark_gray>(ᴇ.ɢ. <white>100,64,200<gray>)"
# ── PVP bot ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── # {bot} — number of bots | {player} — target player name pvp-bot-target-set: "{prefix}<#0079FF>{bot}</#0079FF> <gray>ᴘᴠᴘ ʙᴏᴛ(ꜱ) ᴡɪʟʟ <red>ʜᴜɴᴛ <white>{player}<gray>." player-not-found: "{prefix}<red>ᴘʟᴀʏᴇʀ <white>{player} <red>ɪꜱ ɴᴏᴛ ᴏɴʟɪɴᴇ."
# ── Spawn cooldown ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── # {seconds} — seconds remaining spawn-cooldown: "{prefix}<red>ᴘʟᴇᴀꜱᴇ ᴡᴀɪᴛ <white>{seconds}s<red> ʙᴇꜰᴏʀᴇ ꜱᴘᴀᴡɴɪɴɢ ᴀɡᴀɪɴ."
# ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ # 4 · DELETE # ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# {name} — bot name | {count} — bots deleted delete-success: "{prefix}<#0079FF>{name}</#0079FF> <gray>ʜᴀꜱ ʙᴇᴇɴ <white>ᴅɪꜱᴄᴏɴɴᴇᴄᴛᴇᴅ<gray>." delete-all: "{prefix}<#0079FF>{count}</#0079FF> <gray>ꜰᴀᴋᴇ ᴘʟᴀʏᴇʀ(ꜱ) <white>ᴅɪꜱᴄᴏɴɴᴇᴄᴛᴇᴅ<gray>." delete-none: "{prefix}<red>ɴᴏ ʙᴏᴛꜱ ᴀʀᴇ ᴄᴜʀʀᴇɴᴛʟʏ ᴀᴄᴛɪᴠᴇ." delete-not-found: "{prefix}<red>ɴᴏ ᴀᴄᴛɪᴠᴇ ʙᴏᴛ ɴᴀᴍᴇᴅ <white>{name}<red> ᴡᴀꜱ ꜰᴏᴜɴᴅ." # {count} — number of bots randomly despawned delete-random-success: "{prefix}<#0079FF>{count}</#0079FF> <gray>ʀᴀɴᴅᴏᴍ ꜰᴀᴋᴇ ᴘʟᴀʏᴇʀ(ꜱ) <white>ᴅɪꜱᴄᴏɴɴᴇᴄᴛᴇᴅ<gray>."
# ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ # 5 · LIST # ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
list-none: " <gray>ɴᴏ ʙᴏᴛꜱ ᴀʀᴇ ᴄᴜʀʀᴇɴᴛʟʏ ᴀᴄᴛɪᴠᴇ."
# ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ # 6 · BOT BROADCASTS # ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# {name} — bot display name (colours preserved — do not wrap in extra MiniMessage tags) bot-join: "{prefix}{name} <gray>ᴊᴏɪɴᴇᴅ ᴛʜᴇ ɢᴀᴍᴇ</gray>." bot-leave: "{prefix}{name} <gray>ʟᴇꜰᴛ ᴛʜᴇ ɢᴀᴍᴇ</gray>."
# ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ # 7 · FAKE CHAT # ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# ── Global toggle ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── chat-enabled: "{prefix}<#0079FF>ꜰᴀᴋᴇ ᴄʜᴀᴛ</#0079FF> <gray>ʜᴀꜱ ʙᴇᴇɪɴ <white>ᴇɴᴀʙʟᴇᴅ<gray>." chat-disabled: "{prefix}<#0079FF>ꜰᴀᴋᴇ ᴄʜᴀᴛ</#0079FF> <gray>ʜᴀꜱ ʙᴇᴇɪɴ <white>ᴅɪꜱᴀʙʟᴇᴅ<gray>." chat-status-on: "{prefix}<#0079FF>ꜰᴀᴋᴇ ᴄʜᴀᴛ</#0079FF> <gray>ɪꜱ <white>ᴇɴᴀʙʟᴇᴅ<gray>." chat-status-off: "{prefix}<#0079FF>ꜰᴀᴋᴇ ᴄʜᴀᴛ</#0079FF> <gray>ɪꜱ <white>ᴅɪꜱᴀʙʟᴇᴅ<gray>." chat-invalid: "{prefix}<red>ᴜꜱᴀɢᴇ<gray>: <#0079FF>/fpp chat <on|off|status></#0079FF> <dark_gray>|</dark_gray> <#0079FF>/fpp chat <bot> [on|off|say <msg>|tier <tier>]</#0079FF><gray>."
# ── Per-bot chat management ───────────────────────────────────────────────────── # {name} — bot display name # {message} — message that was forced-sent # {tier} — activity tier name | {tiers} — valid tiers list # {seconds} — mute duration | {enabled} — enabled state | {multiplier} — tier multiplier chat-bot-not-found: "{prefix}<red>ɴᴏ ᴀᴄᴛɪᴠᴇ ʙᴏᴛ ɴᴀᴍᴇᴅ <white>{name}<red>." chat-bot-status-on: "{prefix}<#0079FF>{name}</#0079FF> <gray>ᴄʜᴀᴛ ɪꜱ <white>ᴇɴᴀʙʟᴇᴅ<gray>." chat-bot-status-off: "{prefix}<#0079FF>{name}</#0079FF> <gray>ᴄʜᴀᴛ ɪꜱ <white>ꜱɪʟᴇɴᴄᴇᴅ<gray>." chat-bot-enabled: "{prefix}<#0079FF>{name}</#0079FF> <gray>ᴡɪʟʟ <white>ɴᴏᴡ ᴄʜᴀᴛ<gray>." chat-bot-disabled: "{prefix}<#0079FF>{name}</#0079FF> <gray>ʜᴀꜱ ʙᴇᴇɪɴ <white>ꜱɪʟᴇɴᴄᴇᴅ<gray>." chat-bot-said: "{prefix}<#0079FF>{name}</#0079FF> <gray>ꜱᴀɪᴅ: <white>{message}" chat-bot-info: "{prefix}<#0079FF>{name}</#0079FF> <dark_gray>│ <gray>ᴇɴᴀʙʟᴇᴅ: <white>{enabled} <dark_gray>│ <gray>ᴛɪᴇʀ: <white>{tier} <dark_gray>│ <gray>ᴍᴜʟᴛ: <white>{multiplier}×" chat-bot-muted: "{prefix}<#0079FF>{name}</#0079FF> <gray>ʜᴀꜱ ʙᴇᴇɪɴ <white>ᴘᴇʀᴍᴀɴᴇɴᴛʟʏ ꜱɪʟᴇɴᴄᴇᴅ<gray>." chat-bot-muted-timed: "{prefix}<#0079FF>{name}</#0079FF> <gray>ꜱɪʟᴇɴᴄᴇᴅ ꜰᴏʀ <white>{seconds}ꜱ<gray>." chat-mute-usage: "{prefix}<red>ᴜꜱᴀɢᴇ<gray>: <#0079FF>/fpp chat <bot> mute [seconds]</#0079FF><gray>." chat-bot-tier-set: "{prefix}<#0079FF>{name}</#0079FF><gray>'ꜱ ᴀᴄᴛɪᴠɪᴛʏ ᴛɪᴇʀ ꜱᴇᴛ ᴛᴏ <white>{tier}<gray>." chat-bot-tier-reset: "{prefix}<#0079FF>{name}</#0079FF><gray>'ꜱ ᴀᴄᴛɪᴠɪᴛʏ ᴛɪᴇʀ ʀᴇꜱᴇᴛ ᴛᴏ <white>ʀᴀɴᴅᴏᴍ<gray>." chat-bot-tier-invalid: "{prefix}<red>ɪɴᴠᴀʟɪᴅ ᴛɪᴇʀ '<white>{tier}<red>'. <gray>ᴜꜱᴇ: <white>{tiers}<gray>." chat-say-usage: "{prefix}<red>ᴜꜱᴀɢᴇ<gray>: <#0079FF>/fpp chat <bot> say <message></#0079FF><gray>." chat-tier-usage: "{prefix}<red>ᴜꜱᴀɢᴇ<gray>: <#0079FF>/fpp chat <bot> tier <quiet|passive|normal|active|chatty|reset></#0079FF><gray>."
# ── Bulk bot chat (/fpp chat all …) ─────────────────────────────────────────── # {count} — bots affected | {enabled}/{disabled} — status counts # {tier} — tier name | {message} — message text chat-all-usage: "{prefix}<red>ᴜꜱᴀɢᴇ<gray>: <#0079FF>/fpp chat all <on|off|status|say <msg>|tier <tier>></#0079FF><gray>." chat-all-say-usage: "{prefix}<red>ᴜꜱᴀɢᴇ<gray>: <#0079FF>/fpp chat all say <message></#0079FF><gray>." chat-all-enabled: "{prefix}<gray>ᴀʟʟ <white>{count}<gray> ʙᴏᴛ(ꜱ) ᴡɪʟʟ <white>ɴᴏᴡ ᴄʜᴀᴛ<gray>." chat-all-disabled: "{prefix}<gray>ᴀʟʟ <white>{count}<gray> ʙᴏᴛ(ꜱ) ʜᴀᴠᴇ ʙᴇᴇɪɴ <white>ꜱɪʟᴇɴᴄᴇᴅ<gray>." chat-all-status: "{prefix}<gray>ᴄʜᴀᴛ ᴇɴᴀʙʟᴇᴅ: <white>{enabled}<gray> | ꜱɪʟᴇɴᴄᴇᴅ: <white>{disabled}<gray>." chat-all-said: "{prefix}<gray>ᴍᴇꜱꜱᴀɢᴇ ꜱᴇɴᴛ ᴛʜʀᴏᴜɢʜ <white>{count}<gray> ʙᴏᴛ(ꜱ): <white>{message}" chat-all-tier-set: "{prefix}<gray>ᴀʟʟ ʙᴏᴛꜱ' ᴀᴄᴛɪᴠɪᴛʏ ᴛɪᴇʀ ꜱᴇᴛ ᴛᴏ <white>{tier}<gray>." chat-all-tier-reset: "{prefix}<gray>ᴀʟʟ ʙᴏᴛꜱ' ᴀᴄᴛɪᴠɪᴛʏ ᴛɪᴇʀ ʀᴇꜱᴇᴛ ᴛᴏ <white>ʀᴀɴᴅᴏᴍ<gray>."
# ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ # 8 · INFO / DATABASE # ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# {name} — bot or spawner name queried info-no-records: "{prefix}<red>ɴᴏ ʀᴇᴄᴏʀᴅꜱ ꜰᴏᴜɴᴅ ꜰᴏʀ <white>{name}<red>." info-db-unavailable: "{prefix}<red>ᴅᴀᴛᴀʙᴀꜱᴇ ᴜɴᴀᴠᴀɪʟᴀʙʟᴇ. ᴄʜᴇᴄᴋ ʏᴏᴜʀ ᴄᴏɴꜰɪɡ ᴀɴᴅ ꜱᴇʀᴠᴇʀ ʟᴏɡꜱ."
# ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ # 9 · TELEPORT # ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# {name} — bot name tph-success: "{prefix}<gray>ᴛᴇʟᴇᴘᴏʀᴛᴇᴅ <#0079FF>{name}</#0079FF> <gray>ᴛᴏ ʏᴏᴜ." tph-no-bots: "{prefix}<red>ʏᴏᴜ ʜᴀᴠᴇ ɴᴏ ᴀᴄᴛɪᴠᴇ ʙᴏᴛꜱ."
# ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ # 10 · XP COMMAND # ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# {name} — bot name | {xp} — XP points collected | {seconds} — cooldown duration xp-not-found: "{prefix}<red>ɴᴏ ʙᴏᴛ ɴᴀᴍᴇᴅ <white>{name}<red>." xp-success: "{prefix}<#66CC66>✓</#66CC66> <gray>ᴄᴏʟʟᴇᴄᴛᴇᴅ <#0079FF>{xp}</#0079FF> <gray>xᴘ ꜰʀᴏᴍ <#0079FF>{name}</#0079FF><gray>. <dark_gray>ᴛʜᴇʏ ᴄᴀɴɴᴏᴛ ᴘɪᴄᴋ ᴜᴘ xᴘ ꜰᴏʀ {seconds}ꜱ." xp-no-xp: "{prefix}<gray><#0079FF>{name}</#0079FF> <gray>ʜᴀꜱ ɴᴏ xᴘ." xp-usage: "{prefix}<red>ᴜꜱᴀɢᴇ<gray>: <#0079FF>/fpp xp <bot></#0079FF><gray>." xp-cooldown-active: "{prefix}<red><white>{name}<red> ᴄᴀɴɴᴏᴛ ᴘɪᴄᴋ ᴜᴘ xᴘ ꜰᴏʀ ᴀɴᴏᴛʜᴇʀ <white>{seconds}<red>ꜱ." xp-disabled: "{prefix}<red>xᴘ ꜱʏꜱᴛᴇᴍ ɪꜱ ᴅɪꜱᴀʙʟᴇᴅ ɪɴ ᴄᴏɴꜰɪɡ."
# ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ # 11 · RANK (LuckPerms group assignment) # ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# {bot} — bot display name | {group} — LuckPerms group | {count} — bots assigned rank-no-luckperms: "{prefix}<red>ʟᴜᴄᴋᴘᴇʀᴍꜱ ɪꜱ ɴᴏᴛ ɪɴꜱᴛᴀʟʟᴇᴅ ᴏʀ ᴇɴᴀʙʟᴇᴅ." rank-usage: "{prefix}<red>ᴜꜱᴀɢᴇ<gray>: <#0079FF>/fpp rank <bot> <group|clear></#0079FF><gray> <dark_gray>|</dark_gray> <#0079FF>/fpp rank random <group> [num]</#0079FF><gray> <dark_gray>|</dark_gray> <#0079FF>/fpp rank list</#0079FF><gray>." rank-bot-not-found: "{prefix}<red>ɴᴏ ᴀᴄᴛɪᴠᴇ ʙᴏᴛ ɴᴀᴍᴇᴅ <white>{name}<red>." rank-group-not-found: "{prefix}<red>ʟᴜᴄᴋᴘᴇʀᴍꜱ ɢʀᴏᴜᴘ <white>{group}<red> ᴅᴏᴇꜱ ɴᴏᴛ ᴇxɪꜱᴛ."
# ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ # 12 · ALERT (cross-server broadcast) # ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# {message} — the alert text alert-received: "{prefix}<red><bold>ᴀʟᴇʀᴛ<reset> <dark_gray>» <white>{message}" alert-sent: "{prefix}<#66CC66>✓</#66CC66> <gray>ᴀʟᴇʀᴛ ʙʀᴏᴀᴅᴄᴀꜱᴛᴇᴅ ᴛᴏ <#0079FF>ᴀʟʟ ꜱᴇʀᴠᴇʀꜱ</#0079FF><gray>." alert-usage: "{prefix}<red>ᴜꜱᴀɢᴇ<gray>: <#0079FF>/fpp alert <message></#0079FF><gray>."
# ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ # 13 · SYNC (config synchronisation) # ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# {file} — config file name | {count} — files with local changes sync-not-available: "{prefix}<red>ᴄᴏɴꜰɪɡ ꜱʏɴᴄ ɪꜱ ɴᴏᴛ ᴀᴠᴀɪʟᴀʙʟᴇ <dark_gray>(ɴᴇᴛᴡᴏʀᴋ ᴍᴏᴅᴇ ᴏɴʟʏ<dark_gray>)<red>." sync-usage: "{prefix}<red>ᴜꜱᴀɢᴇ<gray>: <#0079FF>/fpp sync <push|pull|check|status> [file]</#0079FF><gray>." sync-push-success: "{prefix}<#66CC66>✓</#66CC66> <gray>ᴘᴜꜱʜᴇᴅ <#0079FF>{file}</#0079FF> <gray>ᴛᴏ ᴅᴀᴛᴀʙᴀꜱᴇ." sync-push-failed: "{prefix}<red>ꜰᴀɪʟᴇᴅ ᴛᴏ ᴘᴜꜱʜ <white>{file}<red> ᴛᴏ ᴅᴀᴛᴀʙᴀꜱᴇ." sync-push-all-success: "{prefix}<#66CC66>✓</#66CC66> <gray>ᴘᴜꜱʜᴇᴅ <#0079FF>ᴀʟʟ ᴄᴏɴꜰɪɡ ꜰɪʟᴇꜱ</#0079FF> <gray>ᴛᴏ ᴅᴀᴛᴀʙᴀꜱᴇ." sync-pull-success: "{prefix}<#66CC66>✓</#66CC66> <gray>ᴘᴜʟʟᴇᴅ <#0079FF>{file}</#0079FF> <gray>ꜰʀᴏᴍ ᴅᴀᴛᴀʙᴀꜱᴇ." sync-pull-failed: "{prefix}<red>ꜰᴀɪʟᴇᴅ ᴛᴏ ᴘᴜʟʟ <white>{file}<red> ꜰʀᴏᴍ ᴅᴀᴛᴀʙᴀꜱᴇ." sync-pull-all-success: "{prefix}<#66CC66>✓</#66CC66> <gray>ᴘᴜʟʟᴇᴅ <#0079FF>ᴀʟʟ ᴄᴏɴꜰɪɡ ꜰɪʟᴇꜱ</#0079FF> <gray>ꜰʀᴏᴍ ᴅᴀᴛᴀʙᴀꜱᴇ." sync-pull-no-updates: "{prefix}<gray>ᴀʟʟ ᴄᴏɴꜰɪɡ ꜰɪʟᴇꜱ ᴀʀᴇ ᴀʟʀᴇᴀᴅʏ ᴜᴘ ᴛᴏ ᴅᴀᴛᴇ." sync-pull-reload-hint: "{prefix}<gray>ʀᴜɴ <#0079FF>/fpp reload</#0079FF> <gray>ᴛᴏ ᴀᴘᴘʟʏ ᴛʜᴇ ᴄʜᴀɴɢᴇꜱ." sync-check-has-changes: "{prefix}<yellow>⚠<reset> <#0079FF>{file}</#0079FF> <gray>ʜᴀꜱ ʟᴏᴄᴀʟ ᴄʜᴀɴɢᴇꜱ <dark_gray>(ᴅɪꜰꜰᴇʀᴇɴᴛ ꜰʀᴏᴍ ᴅᴀᴛᴀʙᴀꜱᴇ<dark_gray>)." sync-check-no-changes: "{prefix}<#66CC66>✓</#66CC66> <#0079FF>{file}</#0079FF> <gray>ɪꜱ ᴜᴘ ᴛᴏ ᴅᴀᴛᴇ ᴡɪᴛʜ ᴅᴀᴛᴀʙᴀꜱᴇ." sync-check-all-clean: "{prefix}<#66CC66>✓</#66CC66> <gray>ᴀʟʟ ᴄᴏɴꜰɪɡ ꜰɪʟᴇꜱ ꜱʏɴᴄʜʀᴏɴɪᴢᴇᴅ ᴡɪᴛʜ ᴅᴀᴛᴀʙᴀꜱᴇ." sync-check-summary: "{prefix}<gray>ꜱʏɴᴄ ꜱᴛᴀᴛᴜꜱ: <#0079FF>{count}</#0079FF> <gray>ꜰɪʟᴇ(ꜱ) ᴡɪᴛʜ ʟᴏᴄᴀʟ ᴄʜᴀɴɢᴇꜱ." sync-status-no-network: "{prefix}<red>ꜱʏɴᴄ ɪꜱ ᴅɪꜱᴀʙʟᴇᴅ <dark_gray>(ɴᴏᴛ ɪɴ ɴᴇᴛᴡᴏʀᴋ ᴍᴏᴅᴇ<dark_gray>)<red>."
# ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ # 14 · SWAP (session rotation) # ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# {sessions} — active session timers | {offline} — bots waiting to rejoin # {name} — bot name | {count} — bot count # {next} — time until next swap | {min} — min-online floor # {personality} — bot personality | {swaps} — swap cycle count # {time} — time remaining in session swap-enabled: "{prefix}<#0079FF>ꜱᴇꜱɪᴏɴ ʀᴏᴛᴀᴛɪᴏɴ</#0079FF> <gray>ʜᴀꜱ ʙᴇᴇɪɴ <white>ᴇɴᴀʙʟᴇᴅ<gray>." swap-disabled: "{prefix}<#0079FF>ꜱᴇꜱɪᴏɴ ʀᴏᴛᴀᴛɪᴏɴ</#0079FF> <gray>ʜᴀꜱ ʙᴇᴇɪɴ <white>ᴅɪꜱᴀʙʟᴇᴅ<gray>." swap-status-on: "{prefix}<#0079FF>ꜱᴇꜱɪᴏɴ ʀᴏᴛᴀᴛɪᴏɴ</#0079FF> <gray>ɪꜱ <white>ᴇɴᴀʙʟᴇᴅ <dark_gray>│ <gray>ꜱᴇꜱꜱɪᴏɴꜱ: <#0079FF>{sessions}</#0079FF> <dark_gray>│ <gray>ᴏꜰꜰʟɪɴᴇ: <#0079FF>{offline}</#0079FF> <dark_gray>│ <gray>ɴᴇxᴛ: <#0079FF>{next}</#0079FF> <dark_gray>│ <gray>ᴍɪɴ: <#0079FF>{min}</#0079FF>" swap-status-off: "{prefix}<#0079FF>ꜱᴇꜱɪᴏɴ ʀᴏᴛᴀᴛɪᴏɴ</#0079FF> <gray>ɪꜱ <white>ᴅɪꜱᴀʙʟᴇᴅ<gray>." swap-invalid: "{prefix}<red>ᴜꜱᴀɢᴇ<gray>: <#0079FF>/fpp swap</#0079FF> <dark_gray>[<gray>ᴛᴏɢɢʟᴇ<dark_gray>]</dark_gray> <dark_gray>│</dark_gray> <#0079FF>/fpp swap <on|off|status|now <bot>|list|info <bot>></#0079FF><gray>." swap-now-usage: "{prefix}<red>ᴜꜱᴀɢᴇ<gray>: <#0079FF>/fpp swap now <bot></#0079FF><gray>." swap-not-available: "{prefix}<red>ꜱᴡᴀᴘ ꜱʏꜱᴛᴇᴍ ɴᴏᴛ ᴀᴠᴀɪʟᴀʙʟᴇ." swap-now-success: "{prefix}<#66CC66>✓</#66CC66> <#0079FF>{name}</#0079FF> <gray>ɪꜱ ɴᴏᴡ ʟᴇᴀᴠɪɴɢ<gray>." swap-now-failed: "{prefix}<red>ꜰᴀɪʟᴇᴅ ᴛᴏ ꜱᴡᴀᴘ ᴏᴜᴛ <white>{name}<red>." swap-list-empty: "{prefix}<gray>ɴᴏ ʙᴏᴛꜱ ᴄᴜʀʀᴇɴᴛʟʏ ꜱᴄʜᴇᴅᴜʟᴇᴅ ꜰᴏʀ ꜱᴡᴀᴘ." swap-list-header: "{prefix}<gray>ꜱᴄʜᴇᴅᴜʟᴇᴅ ʙᴏᴛꜱ<gray>: <#0079FF>{count}</#0079FF>" swap-list-entry: " <dark_gray>• <#0079FF>{name}</#0079FF> <dark_gray>│ <gray>{personality} <dark_gray>│ <gray>ᴄʏᴄʟᴇꜱ: <white>{swaps} <dark_gray>│ <gray>ʟᴇᴀᴠᴇꜱ: <white>{time}" swap-info-usage: "{prefix}<red>ᴜꜱᴀɢᴇ<gray>: <#0079FF>/fpp swap info <bot></#0079FF><gray>." swap-info-not-found: "{prefix}<red>ɴᴏ ᴀᴄᴛɪᴠᴇ ʙᴏᴛ ɴᴀᴍᴇᴅ <white>{name}<red>." swap-info: "{prefix}<#0079FF>{name}</#0079FF> <dark_gray>│ <gray>ᴘᴇʀꜱᴏɴᴀʟɪᴛʏ: <white>{personality} <dark_gray>│ <gray>ᴄʏᴄʟᴇꜱ: <white>{swaps} <dark_gray>│ <gray>ʟᴇᴀᴠᴇꜱ ɪɴ: <white>{time} <dark_gray>│ <gray>ᴏꜰꜰʟɪɴᴇ ᴡᴀɪᴛɪɴɢ: <white>{offline}"
# ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ # 15 · PEAK HOURS (time-based bot pool scheduling) # ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# {window} — active time window label (e.g. 18:00–22:00) # {fraction} — current target fraction as a % (e.g. 75%) # {target} — target number of bots online # {online} — AFK bots currently online # {swapping} — bots temporarily offline via BotSwapAI session rotation # {sleeping} — bots currently sleeping in the peak-hours pool # {total} — total pool size (online + sleeping + swapping) # {time} — human-readable time until next window (e.g. 1h 20m) # {next_fraction}— fraction of the next window (e.g. 5%) # {count} — number of sleeping bots | {name} — bot name # {timezone} — configured timezone string peaks-enabled: "{prefix}<#0079FF>ᴘᴇᴀᴋ ʜᴏᴜʀꜱ</#0079FF> <gray>ᴇɴᴀʙʟᴇᴅ<gray>." peaks-disabled: "{prefix}<#0079FF>ᴘᴇᴀᴋ ʜᴏᴜʀꜱ</#0079FF> <gray>ᴅɪꜱᴀʙʟᴇᴅ — ᴀʟʟ ꜱʟᴇᴇᴘɪɴɢ ʙᴏᴛꜱ ᴡᴏᴋᴇɴ<gray>." peaks-status-on: "{prefix}<#0079FF>ᴘᴇᴀᴋ ʜᴏᴜʀꜱ</#0079FF> <dark_gray>│ <gray>ᴡɪɴᴅᴏᴡ: <white>{window} <dark_gray>│ <gray>ꜰʀᴀᴄᴛɪᴏɴ: <#0079FF>{fraction}</#0079FF> <dark_gray>│ <gray>ᴛᴀʀɡᴇᴛ: <#0079FF>{target}</#0079FF> <dark_gray>│ <gray>ᴏɴʟɪɴᴇ: <#0079FF>{online}</#0079FF> <dark_gray>│ <gray>ꜱᴡᴀᴘᴘɪɴɢ: <#0079FF>{swapping}</#0079FF> <dark_gray>│ <gray>ꜱʟᴇᴇᴘɪɴɢ: <#0079FF>{sleeping}</#0079FF> <dark_gray>│ <gray>ᴛᴏᴛᴀʟ: <#0079FF>{total}</#0079FF> <dark_gray>│ <gray>ᴛᴢ: <white>{timezone}" peaks-status-off: "{prefix}<#0079FF>ᴘᴇᴀᴋ ʜᴏᴜʀꜱ</#0079FF> <gray>ɪꜱ <white>ᴅɪꜱᴀʙʟᴇᴅ<gray>." peaks-next-window: "{prefix}<gray>ɴᴇxᴛ ᴡɪɴᴅᴏᴡ ɪɴ <#0079FF>{time}</#0079FF> <dark_gray>→ <#0079FF>{next_fraction}</#0079FF> <gray>ᴏꜱ ʙᴏᴛꜱ ᴡɪʟʟ ʙᴇ ᴏɴʟɪɴᴇ<gray>." peaks-no-windows: "{prefix}<gray>ɴᴏ ᴡɪɴᴅᴏᴡꜱ ᴄᴏɴꜰɪɡᴜʀᴇᴅ — ᴇᴅɪᴛ <white>peak-hours.schedule<gray> ɪɴ config.yml." peaks-requires-swap: "{prefix}<red>ᴘᴇᴀᴋ ʜᴏᴜʀꜱ ʀᴇQᴜɪʀᴇꜱ <white>swap.enabled: true<red>. ʀᴜɴ <white>/fpp swap on<red> ꜰɪʀꜱᴛ." peaks-invalid: "{prefix}<red>ᴜꜱᴀɢᴇ<gray>: <#0079FF>/fpp peaks <on|off|status|next|force|list|wake|sleep></#0079FF><gray>." peaks-force-check: "{prefix}<gray>ᴘᴇᴀᴋ-ʜᴏᴜʀꜱ ᴇᴠᴀʟᴜᴀᴛɪᴏɴ ᴛʀɪɡɡᴇʀᴇᴅ." peaks-list-header: "{prefix}<gray>ꜱʟᴇᴇᴘɪɴɢ ʙᴏᴛꜱ <dark_gray>(<#0079FF>{count}</#0079FF>)<gray>:" peaks-list-entry: " <dark_gray>┃ <white>{name} <dark_gray>@ <gray>{world} <dark_gray>[<gray>{x}<dark_gray>, <gray>{y}<dark_gray>, <gray>{z}<dark_gray>]" peaks-list-empty: "{prefix}<gray>ɴᴏ ʙᴏᴛꜱ ᴀʀᴇ ᴄᴜʀʀᴇɴᴛʟʏ ꜱʟᴇᴇᴘɪɴɢ." peaks-wake-success: "{prefix}<gray>ᴡᴏᴋᴇ <white>{name}<gray>." peaks-wake-all: "{prefix}<gray>ᴡᴏᴋᴇ <#0079FF>{count}</#0079FF> ꜱʟᴇᴇᴘɪɴɢ ʙᴏᴛ(ꜱ)." peaks-sleep-success: "{prefix}<gray>ᴘᴜᴛ <white>{name}<gray> ᴛᴏ ꜱʟᴇᴇᴘ." peaks-sleep-failed: "{prefix}<red>ʙᴏᴛ '<white>{name}<red>' ɴᴏᴛ ꜰᴏᴜɴᴅ ᴏʀ ɴᴏᴛ ᴇʟɪɡɪʙʟᴇ." peaks-transition: "{prefix}<#0079FF>ᴘᴇᴀᴋ ᴡɪɴᴅᴏᴡ</#0079FF><gray>: <white>{window} <dark_gray>({fraction} <gray>ᴏꜱ ʙᴏᴛꜱ ᴏɴʟɪɴᴇ<dark_gray>)<gray>."
# ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ # 15.5 · MOVE COMMAND (bot navigation to a player) # ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# {name} — bot display name # {player} — target player name # {count} — waypoint count / index number # {num} — 1-based waypoint index # {x},{y},{z},{world} — waypoint coordinates move-usage: "{prefix}<red>ᴜꜱᴀɢᴇ<gray>: <#0079FF>/fpp move <bot> <player></#0079FF> <gray>ᴏʀ <#0079FF>/fpp move <bot> --wp <route>|--stop</#0079FF><gray>." move-bot-not-found: "{prefix}<red>ɴᴏ ᴀᴄᴛɪᴠᴇ ʙᴏᴛ ɴᴀᴍᴇᴅ <white>{name}<red>." move-bot-not-online: "{prefix}<red>ʙᴏᴛ <white>{name}<red> ɪꜱ ɴᴏᴛ ᴏɴʟɪɴᴇ ᴏʀ ʜᴀꜱ ɴᴏ ʙᴏᴅʏ." move-navigating: "{prefix}<gray>ɴᴀᴠɪɢᴀᴛɪɴɢ <#0079FF>{name}</#0079FF> <gray>ᴛᴏᴡᴀʀᴅ <white>{player}<gray>." move-different-world: "{prefix}<red><#0079FF>{name}</#0079FF> <red>ᴀɴᴅ <white>{player}<red> ᴀʀᴇ ɪɴ ᴅɪꜰꜰᴇʀᴇɴᴛ ᴡᴏʀʟᴅꜱ." # patrol / waypoint messages move-setpos-added: "{prefix}<gray>ᴡᴀʏᴘᴏɪɴᴛ <white>#{count}</white> <gray>ꜱᴀᴠᴇᴅ ꜰᴏʀ <#0079FF>{name}</#0079FF><gray>." move-setpos-player-only: "{prefix}<red>--ꜱᴇᴛᴘᴏꜱ ʀᴇQᴜɪʀᴇꜱ ᴀ ᴘʟᴀʏᴇʀ ᴛᴏ ᴄᴀᴘᴛᴜʀᴇ ᴘᴏꜱɪᴛɪᴏɴ." move-clearpos: "{prefix}<gray>ᴄʟᴇᴀʀᴇᴅ ᴀʟʟ ᴡᴀʏᴘᴏɪɴᴛꜱ ꜰᴏʀ <#0079FF>{name}</#0079FF><gray>." move-clearpos-empty: "{prefix}<red><#0079FF>{name}</#0079FF> <red>ʜᴀꜱ ɴᴏ ᴡᴀʏᴘᴏɪɴᴛꜱ ꜱᴇᴛ." move-patrol-started: "{prefix}<gray><#0079FF>{name}</#0079FF> <gray>ɪꜱ ɴᴏᴡ ᴘᴀᴛʀᴏʟʟɪɴɢ <white>{count}</white> <gray>ᴡᴀʏᴘᴏɪɴᴛꜱ ɪɴ ᴀ ʟᴏᴏᴘ." move-no-waypoints: "{prefix}<red>ɴᴏ ᴡᴀʏᴘᴏɪɴᴛꜱ ꜱᴇᴛ ꜰᴏʀ <#0079FF>{name}</#0079FF><red>. ᴜꜱᴇ <white>/fpp move {name} --setpos</white> ᴛᴏ ᴀᴅᴅ ᴏɴᴇ." move-listpos-header: "{prefix}<gray>ᴡᴀʏᴘᴏɪɴᴛꜱ ꜰᴏʀ <#0079FF>{name}</#0079FF> <gray>(<white>{count}</white>)<gray>:" move-listpos-entry: " <dark_gray>#{num} <white>{x}, {y}, {z} <gray>({world})" move-listpos-empty: "{prefix}<red><#0079FF>{name}</#0079FF> <red>ʜᴀꜱ ɴᴏ ᴡᴀʏᴘᴏɪɴᴛꜱ ꜱᴇᴛ." move-wp-usage: "{prefix}<red>ᴜꜱᴀɢᴇ<gray>: <#0079FF>/fpp move <bot> --wp <route></#0079FF><gray>." move-wp-not-found: "{prefix}<red>ɴᴏ ᴡᴀʏᴘᴏɪɴᴛ ʀᴏᴜᴛᴇ ɴᴀᴍᴇᴅ <white>{name}<red>. ᴜꜱᴇ <white>/fpp wp list</white> ᴛᴏ ꜱᴇᴇ ᴀʟʟ ʀᴏᴜᴛᴇꜱ." move-wp-different-world: "{prefix}<red><#0079FF>{name}</#0079FF><red> ᴀɴᴅ ᴡᴀʏᴘᴏɪɴᴛ <white>{waypoint}<red> ᴀʀᴇ ɪɴ ᴅɪꜰꜰᴇʀᴇɴᴛ ᴡᴏʀʟᴅꜱ." move-wp-started: "{prefix}<gray><#0079FF>{name}</#0079FF><gray> ɪꜱ ɴᴏᴡ ᴘᴀᴛʀᴏʟʟɪɴɢ ʀᴏᴜᴛᴇ <#0079FF>{waypoint}</#0079FF><gray> (<white>{count}</white> ᴘᴏꜱɪᴛɪᴏɴꜱ)<gray>." move-stopped: "{prefix}<gray>ꜱᴛᴏᴘᴘᴇᴅ ɴᴀᴠɪɢᴀᴛɪᴏɴ ꜰᴏʀ <#0079FF>{name}</#0079FF><gray>." move-not-navigating: "{prefix}<red><#0079FF>{name}</#0079FF> <red>ɪꜱ ɴᴏᴛ ᴄᴜʀʀᴇɴᴛʟʏ ɴᴀᴠɪɢᴀᴛɪɴɢ."
# ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ # 15.6 · INVENTORY COMMAND (view/edit bot inventory) # ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# {name} — bot display name inv-usage: "{prefix}<red>ᴜꜱᴀɢᴇ<gray>: <#0079FF>/fpp inventory <bot></#0079FF><gray>." inv-not-found: "{prefix}<red>ɴᴏ ᴀᴄᴛɪᴠᴇ ʙᴏᴛ ɴᴀᴍᴇᴅ <white>{name}<red>." inv-bodyless: "{prefix}<red><#0079FF>{name}</#0079FF> <red>ʜᴀꜱ ɴᴏ ʙᴏᴅʏ <dark_gray>(ʙᴏᴅʏʟᴇꜱꜱ ʙᴏᴛ)<red>." inv-player-only: "{prefix}<red>ᴏɴʟʏ ᴀ ᴘʟᴀʏᴇʀ ᴄᴀɴ ᴏᴘᴇɴ ᴀɴ ɪɴᴠᴇɴᴛᴏʀʏ." inv-opened: "{prefix}<gray>ᴏᴘᴇɴᴇᴅ <#0079FF>{name}</#0079FF><gray>'ꜱ ɪɴᴠᴇɴᴛᴏʀʏ."
# ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ # 15.7 · CMD COMMAND (execute command as bot) # ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# {name} — bot display name | {cmd} — command string (with leading /) cmd-usage: "{prefix}<red>ᴜꜱᴀɢᴇ<gray>: <#0079FF>/fpp cmd <bot> <command...></#0079FF><gray> ᴏʀ <#0079FF>/fpp cmd <bot> --add <command...></#0079FF><gray>." cmd-not-found: "{prefix}<red>ɴᴏ ᴀᴄᴛɪᴠᴇ ʙᴏᴛ ɴᴀᴍᴇᴅ <white>{name}<red>." cmd-bot-offline: "{prefix}<red><#0079FF>{name}</#0079FF><red> ʜᴀꜱ ɴᴏ ᴀᴄᴛɪᴠᴇ ᴘʟᴀʏᴇʀ ʙᴏᴅʏ." cmd-executed: "{prefix}<gray>ᴇxᴇᴄᴜᴛᴇᴅ <#0079FF>{cmd}</#0079FF><gray> ᴀꜱ <#0079FF>{name}</#0079FF><gray>." cmd-failed: "{prefix}<red>ᴄᴏᴍᴍᴀɴᴅ <white>{cmd}<red> ᴅɪᴅ ɴᴏᴛ ʀᴇᴛᴜʀɴ ꜱᴜᴄᴄᴇꜱꜱ ꜰᴏʀ <white>{name}<red>." # --add / --clear / --show feedback cmd-add-usage: "{prefix}<red>ᴜꜱᴀɢᴇ<gray>: <#0079FF>/fpp cmd <bot> --add <command...></#0079FF><gray>." cmd-add-set: "{prefix}<gray>ʀɪɢʜᴛ-ᴄʟɪᴄᴋ ᴄᴏᴍᴍᴀɴᴅ ꜱᴇᴛ ᴏɴ <#0079FF>{name}</#0079FF><gray>: <white>{cmd}<gray>." cmd-add-updated: "{prefix}<gray>ʀɪɢʜᴛ-ᴄʟɪᴄᴋ ᴄᴏᴍᴍᴀɴᴅ ᴜᴘᴅᴀᴛᴇᴅ ꜰᴏʀ <#0079FF>{name}</#0079FF><gray>: <red>{old}</red> <dark_gray>→ <white>{cmd}<gray>." cmd-add-cleared: "{prefix}<gray>ʀɪɢʜᴛ-ᴄʟɪᴄᴋ ᴄᴏᴍᴍᴀɴᴅ ᴄʟᴇᴀʀᴇᴅ ꜰʀᴏᴍ <#0079FF>{name}</#0079FF><gray>." cmd-add-none: "{prefix}<gray>ɴᴏ ʀɪɢʜᴛ-ᴄʟɪᴄᴋ ᴄᴏᴍᴍᴀɴᴅ ɪꜱ ꜱᴇᴛ ᴏɴ <#0079FF>{name}</#0079FF><gray>." cmd-add-show: "{prefix}<gray>ʀɪɢʜᴛ-ᴄʟɪᴄᴋ ᴄᴏᴍᴍᴀɴᴅ ꜰᴏʀ <#0079FF>{name}</#0079FF><gray>: <white>{cmd}<gray>."
# ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ # 15 · MINE COMMAND # ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# /fpp mine <bot> [once|stop] | /fpp mine stop mine-usage: "{prefix}<red>ᴜꜱᴀɢᴇ<gray>: <#0079FF>/fpp mine <bot> [once|stop]</#0079FF><gray> | <#0079FF>/fpp mine stop</#0079FF><gray>." mine-not-found: "{prefix}<red>ʙᴏᴛ <#0079FF>{name}</#0079FF><red> ᴅᴏᴇꜱ ɴᴏᴛ ᴇxɪꜱᴛ." mine-bot-offline: "{prefix}<red><#0079FF>{name}</#0079FF><red> ʜᴀꜱ ɴᴏ ᴀᴄᴛɪᴠᴇ ᴘʟᴀʏᴇʀ ʙᴏᴅʏ." mine-walking: "{prefix}<gray><#0079FF>{name}</#0079FF><gray> ɪꜱ ᴡᴀʟᴋɪɴɢ ᴛᴏ ᴍɪɴɪɴɢ ꜱᴘᴏᴛ..." mine-started: "{prefix}<gray><#0079FF>{name}</#0079FF><gray> ꜱᴛᴀʀᴛᴇᴅ ᴍɪɴɪɴɢ ᴄᴏɴᴛɪɴᴜᴏᴜꜱʟʏ." mine-started-once: "{prefix}<gray><#0079FF>{name}</#0079FF><gray> ᴍɪɴɪɴɢ ᴏɴᴇ ʙʟᴏᴄᴋ..." mine-stopped: "{prefix}<gray><#0079FF>{name}</#0079FF><gray> ꜱᴛᴏᴘᴘᴇᴅ ᴍɪɴɪɴɢ." mine-stopped-all: "{prefix}<gray>ꜱᴛᴏᴘᴘᴇᴅ ᴀʟʟ ʙᴏᴛꜱ ꜰʀᴏᴍ ᴍɪɴɪɴɢ."
# ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ # 16 · USE COMMAND # ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# /fpp use <bot> [once|stop] | /fpp use stop use-usage: "{prefix}<red>ᴜꜱᴀɢᴇ<gray>: <#0079FF>/fpp use <bot> [once|stop]</#0079FF><gray> | <#0079FF>/fpp use stop</#0079FF><gray>." use-not-found: "{prefix}<red>ʙᴏᴛ <#0079FF>{name}</#0079FF><red> ᴅᴏᴇꜱ ɴᴏᴛ ᴇxɪꜱᴛ." use-bot-offline: "{prefix}<red><#0079FF>{name}</#0079FF><red> ʜᴀꜱ ɴᴏ ᴀᴄᴛɪᴠᴇ ᴘʟᴀʏᴇʀ ʙᴏᴅʏ." use-walking: "{prefix}<gray><#0079FF>{name}</#0079FF><gray> ɪꜱ ᴡᴀʟᴋɪɴɢ ᴛᴏ ᴜꜱᴇ ꜱᴘᴏᴛ..." use-started: "{prefix}<gray><#0079FF>{name}</#0079FF><gray> ꜱᴛᴀʀᴛᴇᴅ ʀɪɢʜᴛ-ᴄʟɪᴄᴋɪɴɢ ᴄᴏɴᴛɪɴᴜᴏᴜꜱʟʏ." use-started-once: "{prefix}<gray><#0079FF>{name}</#0079FF><gray> ʀɪɢʜᴛ-ᴄʟɪᴄᴋɪɴɢ ᴏɴᴄᴇ..." use-stopped: "{prefix}<gray><#0079FF>{name}</#0079FF><gray> ꜱᴛᴏᴘᴘᴇᴅ ʀɪɢʜᴛ-ᴄʟɪᴄᴋɪɴɢ." use-stopped-all: "{prefix}<gray>ꜱᴛᴏᴘᴘᴇᴅ ᴀʟʟ ʙᴏᴛꜱ ꜰʀᴏᴍ ʀɪɢʜᴛ-ᴄʟɪᴄᴋɪɴɢ."
# ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ # 17 · UPDATE CHECKER # ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# {version} — detected Minecraft version string (e.g. "1.22.0") # Shown to any sender who tries a /fpp sub-command on an unsupported MC version. version-unsupported: "{prefix}<red>FakePlayerPlugin is <bold>ɴᴏᴛ ꜱᴜᴘᴘᴏʀᴛᴇᴅ</bold> on <white>MC {version}<red>. All commands are disabled. <click:open_url:'https://discord.gg/RfjEJDG2TM'><#0079FF><underlined>Discord</underlined></#0079FF></click>" # Shown to ops / admins on join while the server runs an unsupported version. version-unsupported-admin: "<dark_gray><st>━━━━━━━━━</st> <bold><red>⚠ ᴜɴꜱᴜᴘᴘᴏʀᴛᴇᴅ ᴠᴇʀꜱɪᴏɴ ⚠</red></bold> <dark_gray><st>━━━━━━━━━</st>\n <red>FakePlayerPlugin is <bold>ɴᴏᴛ ꜱᴜᴘᴘᴏʀᴛᴇᴅ</bold> on <white>MC {version}<red>. (Supported: up to <white>MC 1.21.11<red>)\n <gray>All /fpp commands have been disabled until an update is released.\n <gray>If you think this is a bug, contact us: <click:open_url:'https://discord.gg/RfjEJDG2TM'><#0079FF><underlined>Discord</underlined></#0079FF></click><gray>.\n<dark_gray><st>━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━</st>"
